item.title: Teaching | China prepares law that imposes Mandarin as the main language
item.description: China is preparing to pass a law that will make Mandarin the main language of instruction for ethnic minorities. According to the British newspaper Financial Times, the draft Law for the Promotion of Ethnic Unity and Progress is expected to be approved this week during the annual meeting of the Chinese parliament, the National People's Congress. The diploma establishes that Mandarin will become the main language of instruction in schools and universities attended by minorities, allowing languages ÔÇőÔÇősuch as Tibetan, Uighur or Mongolian to be taught only as a second language, no longer being able to be used in core subjects. The text also provides for legal action against individuals or organizations, inside or outside China, that ÔÇťundermine national unityÔÇŁ or incite ÔÇťseparatismÔÇŁ, and states that ethnic unity ÔÇťshould not be interfered with by external forces under the pretext of ethnicity, religion or human rightsÔÇŁ. Analysts cited by the newspaper consider that the new law represents a structural change in the State's approach to minorities. For critics, the legislation is part of a broader effort at cultural assimilation that has been intensifying under the leadership of Xi Jinping, in a country where the Han majority represents around 90 percent of the population of 1.4 billion inhabitants, but which officially recognizes 56 ethnic groups and several languages ÔÇőÔÇőwith their own writing systems. Neil Thomas, a researcher at the Asia Society's China Analysis Center, said the law "will expand the legal basis for restricting religious, cultural and political activities among minority groups." Wang Yanzhong, a researcher linked to the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, argued that the diploma aims to provide legal safeguards for the Communist Party's work on ethnic matters and contribute to ÔÇťmaintaining the security and stability of border regions and ethnic areasÔÇŁ. The academic also argued that Mandarin offers greater educational and professional advantages, arguing that the number of speakers of minority languages ÔÇőÔÇőis decreasing among younger generations.

item.title: IPIM | Trade fair for Portuguese-speaking countries in October
item.description: The Government announced yesterday that it will organize a trade fair in October dedicated only to Portuguese-speaking countries, instead of the usual event that brings together companies from China and Portuguese-speaking markets. The Macau Trade and Investment Promotion Institute (IPIM) launched a competition to coordinate the Exhibition of Products and Services of Portuguese Speaking Countries (Macau) 2026 (PLPEX), scheduled for between 21 and 24 October. The specifications foresee that PLPEX will occupy an area of ÔÇőÔÇősix thousand square meters, with around 200 stands for exhibitors ÔÇťin line with Macau's positioning as a Sino-Lusophone platformÔÇŁ. The fair aims to attract at least 200 exhibitors, of which 10 percent must be local companies, 10 percent from Spain and 80 percent from Portuguese-speaking markets, ÔÇťit being mandatory to attract the participation of companies from all nine Portuguese-speaking countriesÔÇŁ. The agricultural sector must represent at least 30 percent of all exhibitors, cross-border electronic commerce 20 percent and the blue economy, linked to the sea, at least 10 percent, states in the specifications. The PLPEX organizer must also create a campaign to promote the fair in Portugal and Spain. Lusa questioned IPIM about whether the new fair will replace the China-Portuguese Speaking Countries Economic and Commercial Exhibition (C-PLPEX), which was organized in October 2025, in parallel with the Macau International Fair (MIF, its acronym in English), but did not receive any response.

item.title: Taiwan | Those responsible for the shopping center that exploded deny accusations
item.description: A trial is taking place in Taiwan that puts two people responsible for the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi shopping center in Taichung on the stand, where a gas explosion occurred in February last year, which resulted in five deaths and 38 injuries. The victims included three Macao SAR residents who lost their lives and four who were injured. The trial started yesterday, and the shopping center manager, the deputy manager of the parent company for the Taichung region (which has several shopping centers) and an employee pleaded not guilty, denying charges of negligent homicide, negligent wrongdoing and violating the occupational health and safety law. The shopping center in question is responsible for the largest revenue and flow of visitors for the entire group, which has centers in several locations in Taiwan. According to the Taiwanese newspaper Liberty Times, the prosecution argued that due to the size of the space, public safety should be implemented with the highest standards of rigor, but that this did not happen, with the company ignoring legal requirements and taking risks in the works for financial reasons that led to the explosion. The investigation revealed that those responsible did not request the necessary legal authorizations to carry out the work, a fire prevention plan was not drawn up, and the gas supply was not interrupted during the work. The trial continues this afternoon, with interviews with more defendants, 13 in total.

item.title: Art without borders | Registration open for ÔÇťWorkshops for ChildrenÔÇŁ
item.description: Registration is open until March 22 to participate in seven sessions of artistic workshops, aimed at children and young people aged 4 to 16, under the ÔÇťArt Without Borders ÔÇô Workshops for ChildrenÔÇŁ program that takes place between April and June at the Macau Museum of Art (MAM). The activities are exclusive to ÔÇťFriends of MAMÔÇŁ, that is, you must join the club. Registration for workshops and membership of the ÔÇťFriends of MAMÔÇŁ can be done through the Macau Single Account. Each workshop lasts 12 hours and costs 240 patacas. The places that will host these activities are Espa├žo Zero, at MAM and the Art Square at the Museum of Gifts on the Transfer of Sovereignty of Macau. The Cultural Institute points out that the organization of these events aims to encourage young people ÔÇťto observe and imagine traditional aesthetics, as well as to develop creative practice in contemporary art through different forms of expression, allowing them to experience the infinite possibilities of artÔÇŁ. In conjunction with the theme of the exhibition dedicated to landscape painting from the Ming and Qing dynasties, this series of workshops will take children and young people of different ages ÔÇťon an artistic journey through time, following the trail of the ancient masters of Chinese paintingÔÇŁ. ÔÇťPainting and Imagination ÔÇô Creative Art Workshop for ChildrenÔÇŁ, conducted in Cantonese or English (a total of five sessions), was designed for children aged between 5 and 10 and will have as its theme natural landscapes that will be reinterpreted through paintings with Indian ink and collages. The ÔÇťImaginary Landscape Painting ÔÇô Creative Workshop for Young PeopleÔÇŁ workshop is aimed at teenagers aged 11 to 16. Finally, a workshop will be organized for parents and children aged between 4 and 6.

item.title: ÔÇťThree regionsÔÇŁ events: from pioneering to institutionalization
item.description:
By Manuel Silv├ęrio ÔÇô former president of the Sports Institute and co-founder of the Macau Olympic Committee ┬á The written question from Deputy Leong Sun Iok raises a timely and, above all, concrete point: how to transform recent experiences ÔÇö in particular the cycling event that linked Macau, Hong Kong and Guangdong ÔÇö into institutionalized advantages, capable of reinforcing regional cooperation and consolidating Macau as a Sports City. The merit of this position also lies in the profile of those who place it. Leong Sun Iok is a young deputy, close to the field, who regularly accompanies athletes and practitioners. And it is not insignificant that it is linked to the Federation of Workers' Associations of Macau (FAOM), an institution with strong social and sporting traditions, where many young people ÔÇö and also ÔÇťanonymousÔÇŁ practitioners ÔÇö have found, over decades, space to train, compete and create active lifestyle habits. Just remember the emblematic Campo dos Oper├írios, in the heart of the city, next to the Portuguese School, where the Grand Lisboa stands today ÔÇö a symbol of how Macau has changed, but where the sporting memory remains. The central issue is simple: pioneering experiences are important, but what counts is what happens afterwards. And this is where the deputy's question is decisive: when introducing or hosting international events in the future, will there be conditions to take advantage of the experience gained in the National Games? Will there be a mechanism to facilitate authorizations and coordination with Guangdong and Hong Kong? ┬á What the Government might do ÔÇťnextÔÇŁ (realistic forecast) Without guessing too much, there are a set of steps that the Government could realistically take to transform these experiences into a permanent capacity:
ÔüáCreate an interdepartmental working group (security, traffic, tourism, sport, border issues and health), with regular meetings and a clear decision-making method. Establish a ÔÇťgreen channelÔÇŁ for ÔÇťthree-regionÔÇŁ events, with defined criteria, fixed deadlines and a predictable administrative circuit. ÔüáConsolidate an annual or multi-annual calendar with 1ÔÇô2 anchor events (e.g. a cross-border cycling race and a half marathon/border crossing race). Strengthen cooperation with Guangdong and Hong Kong in a technical format, not just political: operational protocols, contingency plans and coordination between teams on the ground. ÔüáSecure partners with operational capacity and sponsorship, including robust private sector participation, where this increases efficiency and scale.
The risk is known: everything will be left with ÔÇťgood intentionsÔÇŁ if there is no owner of the process ÔÇö a leading entity ÔÇö and execution goals with accountability. If Macau really wants to gain scale and regional relevance through sport, the path passes less through announcements and more through mechanisms, deadlines, leadership and execution. Deputy Leong Sun Iok's interpellation, in this sense, is a good starting point to ask the right question ÔÇö and, above all, to demand operational answers.

item.title: CPPCC | Open main annual political meeting
item.description: China yesterday kicked off its main annual political meeting with the opening of the session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), the consultative body that precedes the inauguration, on Thursday, of the National People's Assembly, a legislative body. Chinese President Xi Jinping participated in the inaugural session held at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, accompanied by other members of the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). During the meeting, the work on the agenda was approved and Wang Huning, considered one of the regime's main ideologues and close to Xi, presented the activity report of the consultative body's standing committee. The opening of the so-called ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ, China's biggest annual political event, takes place at the beginning of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), which will define the country's economic and strategic priorities for the next five years. Tomorrow, Prime Minister Li Qiang is expected to announce the economic growth target for 2026, which, according to analysts, could be between 4.5 percent and 5 percent. The annual meeting also takes place after the expulsion, in recent weeks, of several senior military officials from both the National Popular Assembly and the consultative body itself, as part of the anti-corruption campaign that has reduced the leadership of the armed forces. The Defense budget, which in last year's session increased by 7.2 percent year-on-year, and political messages aimed at the Armed Forces should also focus part of the attention during the debates. ┬á Global tensions The session is also taking place in a context of growing international tensions, following attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, and before a planned visit by US President Donald Trump to China, which has not yet been officially confirmed by Beijing. In the first contacts with the local press, some members of the consultative body highlighted topics such as industrial and technological modernization. The president of car manufacturer Geely, Li Shufu, called, in statements cited by The Paper newspaper, to avoid ÔÇťsenseless competitionÔÇŁ in the electric vehicle sector, while other delegates highlighted advances in quantum technology and the need to accelerate the regulation of autonomous vehicles and humanoid robotics. During the session of the legislative body, Foreign Minister Wang Yi is expected to hold his usual annual press conference, an event often used to anticipate the tone of Chinese diplomacy in an increasingly volatile international context.

item.title: IC | April will feature a comedy festival, reading promotion and sports carnival
item.description: With the Macau International Parade scheduled for the end of the month, Macau is preparing to organize a series of events in April, including the third edition of the Macau International Comedy Festival, with activities in Macau and Hengqin. The Cultural Institute is also preparing reading month for April Although the March procession is still taking place in the churchyard, the Cultural Institute (IC) is dealing with ÔÇťlarge-scale eventsÔÇŁ that will affect Macau's cultural agenda in April, a poster that was on the table at the meeting of the Consultative Council for Cultural Development, which took place on Tuesday. Next month, the third edition of the Macau International Comedy Festival will be organized, with an extension to Hengqin, complying with the ÔÇťÔÇśOne Festival, Two LocationsÔÇÖ formatÔÇŁ. Still far from presenting the poster, the IC points out that the comedy festival will be an ÔÇťinternational level eventÔÇŁ that will culturally enrich the Greater Bay Area and ÔÇťpromote regional cultural exchangeÔÇŁ. Fulfilling the objective of taking advantage of sport to create cultural and tourist attractions, the IC will launch the ÔÇťSports and Cultural CarnivalÔÇŁ in April, which will provide ÔÇťexperiences with cultural and sporting themes held in World Heritage sites and in communitiesÔÇŁ. The event will include the traditional Dragon Boat Regatta. In another aspect, the IC will focus on promoting literacy, designating April as the ÔÇťMonth of Reading Together throughout the City of MacauÔÇŁ, in collaboration with the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services, the Institute of Social Action and civil organizations. As such, ÔÇťa series of activities to promote reading will be organized during the month of April, with the aim of creating a good reading environment for everyone and deepening the construction of a ÔÇśCity of ReadingÔÇÖ, points out the IC. It is important to highlight that the month of reading follows national and international initiatives, with the Central Government designating the fourth week of April each year as ÔÇťReading Week for AllÔÇŁ, in addition to the celebration of World Book Day on April 23rd. ┬á Make the future Another of the points discussed at the meeting in terms of events was the Macau 2026 International Parade, which takes advantage of ÔÇťthe uniqueness of the fusion between East and WestÔÇŁ of the territory. The parade, which takes place on March 29th, this year has the theme ÔÇťThe Maritime Silk Road as a bridge for cultural exchangeÔÇŁ. The event will bring together ÔÇťartistic groups from all over the world and local artists who will dance and sing in dazzling costumes through the busy streets, showcasing the unique character of the Historic Center of MacauÔÇŁ, predicts the IC. The agenda of the meeting also included the presentation of the ÔÇťTraining Program for Young Macau MusiciansÔÇŁ organized by the Macau Orchestra Society, which aims to train musical talents and provide a platform for participation in professional orchestras. The training program also aims to increase employment opportunities for Macau's musical talents in professional orchestras, through the study of Western instruments or traditional Chinese music, contributing to building a reserve of local artistic talents.

item.title: Iran | Oil and closure of the Strait of Hormuz inflate Portuguese products
item.description: The consequences of the war in Iran will be felt in the prices of products imported from Europe, including Portugal. Maritime cargo transportation could take more than three months, whereas in the past just one month was enough. The Macau Suppliers Association also warns of the rise in oil prices and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz ┬á In addition to the blood shed in the Middle East, the offensive in the United States and Israel against Iran, and Tehran's response will have repercussions on the global economy, with Macau not being immune, despite the lessons learned during the pandemic. The president of the Macau Suppliers Union Association, Ip Sio Man, predicts that the prices of imported products will rise, with a particular impact on trade between Europe and Asia via maritime transport. Speaking to the newspaper Ou Mun, the representative stated that the war in the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to navigation will further delay maritime cargo transport that fuels commercial relations between Europe and Asia. The official indicates that before the tensions in the Red Sea, caused by the blockade of the Houthis in Yemen in response to the genocide in Gaza, the transport of cargo ships between Europe and Asia had already increased from one to two months. With the new offensive and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, transport could exceed three months. Among the products imported by Macau that could be most affected, Ip Sio Man highlighted Portuguese wine, olive oil and canned products. Containers and shelves The economic effect of the conflict will also be felt in the increase in the price of oil, which in turn will push up inflation in transport costs. The association leader points out that currently a cargo container costs 10 thousand patacas, but that this value will certainly rise, increased by fears of exchange rate volatility. All these constraints will be felt even before the products reach the shelves of supermarkets and commercial surfaces. Transport delays will force traders to expand their inventory and the storage cycle could go from two to three months to a period that could reach half a year. This disruption will result in increased storage costs. Given all these factors, Ip Sio Man concludes that an increase in the prices of imported products is inevitable. It is recalled that last year, the unit value index of exports rose 1.9 percent in annual terms, which was the highest increase in the last 12 years. The tariff war also comes into these accounts. Ip Sio Man claims that domestic production costs in the United States have increased with Donald Trump's tariffs, increasing pressure on manufacturing industries globally and making international trade and imports more expensive. However, the official highlights that European products represent a relatively small share of the Macau market, excluding luxury goods and dairy products (which occupy second place in terms of place of origin). In relation to this panorama, Ip Sio Man states that following the Covid-19 pandemic, many local suppliers were forced to decentralize their supply chains, looking for closer production sources. Therefore, despite maintaining European product sources, Macau suppliers began to purchase more dairy products in Australia, New Zealand and Mainland China, softening the impact of the European logistics chain.

item.title: Fuels | Portal shows prices with opposite trends
item.description: The information on the Consumer Council portal about vehicles shows prices that go months without being updated. However, the three vehicle fuels that allow comparisons to be made with the days before and during the war in Iran show variations to suit all tastes. ┬á Following the bombings of Iran by the United States and Israel, the price of oil has increased. However, the little information available on the Consumer Council (CC) portal about fuels shows a market with opposite variations. Despite making data on fuel prices available, the Consumer Council indicates that the update is carried out irregularly. Therefore, of the 11 fuels presented on the portal, it is only possible to compare three types, taking into account the price in the week before the bombings and in the days following. In the remaining cases, prices have not been updated for months or weeks. In the case of unleaded gasoline sold by Nam Kwong Oil, the updated price on February 28, a Saturday, showed that a liter was being sold for 14.20 patacas. This is the price without any discount and applied to all the brand's supply points in the territory. However, with discounts, the price could drop to 12.50 patacas per liter. The price update yesterday, March 4, maintained the original price at 14.20 patacas per liter. However, the gas station's special Wednesday promotion allowed you to pay just 11.82 patacas. The remaining prices for unleaded gasoline from the Shell, Esso and Caltex brands were updated the day before yesterday and yesterday. However, the previous updates had been made on December 15th, October 24th and July 6th, respectively, so it is not possible to make comparisons. In the case of Total, yesterday at noon, the price had not been updated since July 11th. ┬á Go up and down In relation to premium gasoline, prices registered opposite movements. According to the CC portal, only two brands are available on the market: Shell and Esso. In the case of the first, on February 26th, Thursday, the original price of fuel, that is, without discounts, at all eight stations was 16.61 patacas per liter. However, yesterday's update shows a reduction in the price of a liter of 8.2 percent, to 15.25 patacas per liter. In the case of Esso, on February 25th premium gasoline became more expensive. The price on February 25th was 16.50 patacas per liter and rose to 16.90 patacas per liter yesterday, an increase of 1.8 percent, or 40 cents. The portal also shows that it is not possible to make any comparison of diesel price movements. According to official information there are five brands on the market, Total, Shell, Esso, Caltex and Nam Kwong Oil. Shell and Catelx prices were updated on Tuesday to values ÔÇőÔÇőbetween 16.76 patacas per liter and 14.06/15.25 patacas per liter. However, the previous updates to these prices took place on July 15th and July 6th, respectively.

item.title: Income | JLL expects prices to stabilize
item.description: After prices fell ÔÇťsharplyÔÇŁ last year, real estate agency JLL points out that rents should stabilize throughout the year, both in terms of housing and commercial spaces. ┬á A market forecast released yesterday by real estate consultancy JLL indicates that rents for housing and commercial spaces in Macau are expected to stabilize this year, even with asset values ÔÇőÔÇőstill under pressure. In a report, the company indicates that residential prices, which fell sharply in 2025, are expected to remain stable in 2026, following government measures to ease mortgage burdens, including stamp duty exemptions and easing loan-to-property value ratios. Banks also cut interest rates late last year, helping to sustain demand. ÔÇťProperty developers reduced prices to boost sales last year and, with supportive policies now in place, the residential market should stabilize in the short term,ÔÇŁ said JLL Senior Director of Valuation and Risk in Macau, Mark Wong. ÔÇťBut limited long-term demand and the absence of major infrastructure projects will continue to weigh on the sector,ÔÇŁ he added, according to the report. The data shows that the equity value of housing fell 16.5 percent in 2025, while that of luxury properties fell 14.7 percent. Rents for conventional units fell 10.3 percent, although rents for high-end residences rose 1.1 percent. JLL predicts that both income and asset values ÔÇőÔÇőwill remain stable in 2026. The office market also faced difficulties last year, with general rents falling 3.4 percent and asset values ÔÇőÔÇőfalling 7.9 percent. The unemployment rate rose to 15.3 percent, reflecting weak demand. ┬á Falling asset value JLL ÔÇô the report also reads ÔÇô expects rents to stabilize in 2026, although asset value could fall by up to 5 percent. The retail sector also experienced pressure, with total sales falling 5.4 percent between January and September last year. Rents fell 0.9 percent in 2025. Although tourist areas are beginning to attract investors again, neighborhood stores remain under pressure due to high loan default rates. JLL predicts that retail asset values ÔÇőÔÇőcould fall another 5 percent this year. Macau's economy showed signs of resilience in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) growing 4.7 percent to 415.32 billion patacas and gaming revenue rising 9.1 percent to 247.4 billion patacas. Gaming revenue from the VIP segment increased by 24.1 percent to MOP67.98 billion. Visitor arrivals grew by 14.7 percent last year, to 40 million, driven mainly by tourists from the Interior. Despite the recovery in tourism and gaming, Wong warned of persistent structural challenges. ÔÇťEconomic growth has returned to pre-pandemic levels, but the drivers are concentrated in just a few industries,ÔÇŁ he said. ÔÇťSentiment in the commercial market is weak and the closure of satellite casinos will continue to pose challenges,ÔÇŁ he said.

item.title: ÔÇťBeat. RespirationÔÇŁ, a concert to listen to at CCM this Friday
item.description: The Macau Chinese Orchestra (OCM) takes the stage this Friday in the small auditorium of the Macau Cultural Center (CCM) as part of the show ÔÇťBatida. Respira├ž├úoÔÇŁ, which highlights ÔÇťthe unique fascination of wind and percussion instrumentsÔÇŁ. According to a note from the Cultural Institute (IC), this Friday's show, which starts at 7:45 pm, will be directed by the OCM's musical director and main conductor, Zhang Lie, and will feature the participation of dizi performer, Zhang Weiliang, as artistic director. ÔÇťA skillful dialogue between the orchestraÔÇÖs wind and percussion sectionsÔÇŁ is promised in this concert, which features ÔÇťa mix of resonant percussion and fluid winds, revealing the fascination of Chinese instrumental musicÔÇŁ. Zhang Weiliang is described as a ÔÇťtrue master of stage performance, composition and music education, as well as a pioneer of ÔÇśnew folk musicÔÇÖÔÇŁ. Furthermore, he is a personality from the world of music who has performed ÔÇťsome of the soundtracks most beloved by the publicÔÇŁ for classic Chinese films, such as ÔÇťGoodbye My ConcubineÔÇŁ, ÔÇťJu DouÔÇŁ and ÔÇťRed LanternÔÇŁ. Traditional compliment The concert will consist of a repertoire of traditional folk songs and modern pieces with new musical arrangements, including compositions such as ÔÇťHanging the Red LanternsÔÇŁ, ÔÇťFestival das LanternasÔÇŁ, ÔÇťDespedida FestivaÔÇŁ, ÔÇťL├ígrimas pela Flores e Felicita├ž├ÁesÔÇŁ, which make their debut in the territory. The OCM wind and percussion groups will also present ÔÇťO Rio Amarelo Jorra do C├ęuÔÇŁ, an original piece by Zhang Lie, while the OCM suona performer, Tian Ding, will perform ÔÇťAo Nascer do SolÔÇŁ, a work of his own. Tickets are now on sale and cost between 150 and 200 patacas. This concert is part of the OCM 2025-26 Concert Season.

item.title: China / USA | Reinforcement of investment before TrumpÔÇÖs arrival
item.description: China and the United States are discussing ways to reactivate reciprocal investment as part of preparations for the US President's planned visit to Beijing, the Hong Kong newspaper South China Morning Post reported yesterday. Citing anonymous sources, the newspaper said that the signing of agreements in the area of ÔÇőÔÇőinvestment could be one of the few tangible results of the trip, announced by Washington for March 31 to April 2, but not yet officially confirmed by Beijing. Uncertainty surrounding the visit and possible results has increased in recent days, following attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran, a country of which China is the main trading partner and largest buyer of oil, as well as one of its most important diplomatic supports. According to the newspaper, although there are no specific agreements yet, both Beijing and Washington have expressed interest in structures such as joint companies with clear governance models, licensing agreements or formats with less exposure in terms of intellectual property, capable of resisting political and regulatory scrutiny. One of the sources cited points to the agreement reached in 2023 between Ford and the Chinese CATL, the world's largest manufacturer of batteries for electric vehicles, as a ÔÇťpossible modelÔÇŁ, which allowed the North American company to license lithium iron phosphate battery technology for use in the United States. Among the points of friction, North American representatives insisted on the need to improve access to the Chinese market, where foreign companies have been denouncing unequal treatment for years. Beijing called for greater protection for its investments in the face of losses and withdrawals driven by greater scrutiny and tariffs, also addressing initial public offerings by Chinese companies in North American markets. The United States has traditionally been one of the main destinations for Chinese foreign investment, which reached a maximum of US$17 billion in 2016, falling to around US$6.6 billion in 2024. In total, China invested more than US$90 billion in the world's largest economy. According to data released by Beijing, US investment in China fell 18.5 percent in 2024, to around US$2.7 billion. According to one of the sources, it was conveyed that Chinese investment is welcome, as long as it is in sectors considered non-sensitive. However, for this to happen, it would be necessary to ÔÇťchange the narrativeÔÇŁ and mitigate the discourse of the ÔÇťChinese threatÔÇŁ dominant in the United States. New round Last week, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce announced the holding of a sixth round of trade negotiations with Washington. Although the date and location have not been confirmed, the talks are expected to serve as a precursor to Trump's visit. The South China Morning Post also said that the trip could allow the extension of the one-year trade truce agreed in October between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, which helped to temporarily ease tensions generated by the tariff escalation that began after the Republican's return to the White House.

item.title: Real Estate | Association wants subsidy when buying first home
item.description: The Macau Property Development Commercial Association argues that the Government should subsidize the payment of interest rates on credits for purchasing a first home, covering 2 percent of this interest. The president of the association, Lok Wai Tak, understands that the measures approved by the Government, such as tax benefits and relaxation of maximum mortgage limits, were not enough to stop the fall in the real estate market and that more policies are needed for the sector to overcome the challenges it faces. In statements cited by the newspaper Ou Mun, the association leader also indicated that the prevailing sentiment in the market continues to be caution, while property sales and prices continue on a downward trend. As such, he called on the Government to take into account the real estate reality of Hong Kong, one of the most speculative markets in the world. The president of the International Real Estate Federation, Che Chan U, also pointed to the weakness of the global economy and the difficulties of small and medium-sized local companies as a scenario that does not help confidence in the real estate market. The leader is hopeful that the Sam Hou Fai Executive will introduce a residence-by-investment policy to stabilize property prices.

item.title: Ana Cristina Alves
item.description: Ana Cristina Alves ÔÇô Assistant Researcher and Coordinator of the Educational Service of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center The popular myth of origin ÔÇťPangu Separates the HeavenÔÇŁ (šŤśňĆĄň╝ÇňĄęP├íngăö k─üi ti─ün) dates back to the Three Kingdoms Period (ńŞëňŤŻ, 220-280) and its Three-Five Calendar Record (ŃÇŐńŞëń║öňÄćš║¬ŃÇő)2, according to data from the online encyclopedia Baike.Baidu, where the Hun Tian cosmological theory (ÔÇťŠÁĹňĄęŔ»┤ÔÇŁ) is exposed, which means ÔÇťtheory of the Celestial GlobeÔÇŁ or, in a less literal translation, ÔÇťtheory of the cosmic eggÔÇŁÔÇŁ3: The Three Five Calendar Record reads "Heaven and Earth were linked like an egg, giving rise to Pangu. In Hun Tian theory, the celestial sphere surrounds the earth, and the center where Heaven and Earth meet is known as 'the geographic center'. It was in Luoyang that Pangu was born, at that time Heaven and Earth had not yet separated, the universe was a chaotic mixture. Pangu slept in this chaos for 18 thousand years. One day, suddenly He woke up. All around him, there was pitch darkness. He raised a large ax with which he fought against the darkness. It dissipated with an immense noise and gradually became the Earth. After Heaven and Earth had separated, the titan feared that they would unite again, so that this would never be possible, he placed his head against Heaven, leaving his feet firmly planted on Earth. And that's how Heaven rose upwards, moving three meters away from Earth every day. For many years he too followed the growth of Heaven, ever higher and more distant. When Heaven and Earth were fully formed, Pangu fell exhausted. After his fall, his body underwent the greatest changes: His breath became the wind and clouds of the four seasons; the voice in thunderous thunder; eyes on the sun and moon, the four cardinal points of the Earth, east, west, south and north; the muscles and skin across the vast expanse of the Earth; the blood in small and large rivers always flowing and the sweat, in the rain and dew that nourish the ten thousand beings. ŠŹ«ŃÇŐńŞëń║öňÄćš║¬ŃÇőŔ«░ŔŻŻ´╝îÔÇťňĄęňť░ŠÁĹŠ▓îňŽéÚŞíňşÉ´╝îšŤśňĆĄšöčňůÂńŞşÔÇŁ ŃÇéŠÁĹňĄęŔ»┤ńŞş´╝îšÉâňú│šŐÂšÜäňĄęňîůšŁÇňť░´╝îňĄęńŞÄňť░šÜäńŞşň┐âň░▒Šś»ÔÇťňť░ńŞşÔÇŁ´╝îňŹ│Š┤ŤÚś│´╝îšŤśňĆĄšöčń║ÄŠşĄŃÇéňŻôŠŚÂňĄęňĺîňť░Ŕ┐śŠ▓íŠťëňłćň╝Ç´╝îň«çň«ÖńŞÇšëçŃÇéšŤśňĆĄňťĘŔ┐ÖńŞ¬ŠĚĚŠ▓îšÜäň«çň«ÖńŞşšŁíń║ćńŞÇńŞçňůźňŹâň╣┤ŃÇéŠťëńŞÇňĄę´╝îšŤśňĆĄš¬üšäÂÚćĺń║ćŃÇéń╗ľŔžüňĹĘňŤ┤ńŞÇšëçŠ╝ćÚ╗Ĺ´╝îń╗ľň░▒ŠŐíŔÁĚňĄžŠľžňĄ┤´╝îŠťŁšť╝ňëŹšÜäÚ╗ĹŠÜŚšîŤňŐłŔ┐çňÄ╗ń║ćŃÇéňĆ¬ňÉČńŞÇňú░ňĚĘňôŹ´╝îńŞÇšëçÚ╗ĹŠÜŚšÜäńŞťŔą┐ŠŞÉŠŞÉňłćŠĽúň╝Çń║ćŃÇéŔŻ╗ŠŞůšÜäńŞťŔą┐š╝ôš╝ôńŞŐňŹç´╝îňĆśŠłÉń║ćňĄę´╝ŤÚçŹŠÁŐšÜäńŞťŔą┐ŠůóŠůóńŞőÚÖŹ´╝îňĆśŠłÉń║ćňť░ŃÇé ňĄęňĺîňť░ňłćň╝Çń╗ąňÉÄ´╝îšŤśňĆĄŠÇĽň«âń╗ČŔ┐śń╝ÜňÉłňťĘńŞÇŔÁĚ´╝îńż┐ňĄ┤ÚíÂšŁÇňĄę´╝îŔäÜŔ╣ČšŁÇňť░ŃÇéňĄęŠ»ĆňĄęňŹçÚźśńŞÇńŞł´╝îšŤśňĆĄń╣čÚÜĆšŁÇňĄęŔÂŐÚĽ┐ŔÂŐÚźśŃÇéŔ┐ÖŠáĚńŞŹščąŔ┐çňĄÜň░Ĺň╣┤´╝îňĄęňĺîňť░ÚÇÉŠŞÉŠłÉňŻóń║ć´╝îšŤśňĆĄń╣čš┤»ňżŚňÇĺńŞőŠŁąń║ćŃÇé šŤśňĆĄňÇĺńŞőňÉÄ´╝îń╗ľňĹ╝ňç║šÜäŠ░öŠü»´╝îňĆśŠłÉń║ćňŤŤňşúšÜäÚúÄňĺîń║Ĺ´╝Ťń╗ľňĆĹňç║šÜäňú░Úč│´╝îňîľńŻťń║ćÚÜćÚÜćšÜäÚŤĚňú░:šÜäňŤŤŔéó, Article ÔÇâ ÔÇâJo├úo Marcelo Martins in A Gram├ítica Universal do Mito (2023, 142) draws attention to the various etymological meanings of the name of Pangu (šŤśňĆĄ), which, in a possible reading, is close to the phonetics of the word ┬źcaba├ža┬╗, P├íoh├╣ (ňîĆšôá), which in Chinese culture has the symbolic meaning of reproduction of life, there are still other interpretative possibilities surrounding the name of this great titan, such as that of a ┬źspiral antiquity┬╗ (Ibidem), because one of the sinograms is ňĆĄ (găö), which means ┬źancient┬╗, while the other šŤś (p├ín), can be translated as ┬źspiral┬╗. The truth is that this Chinese myth of the cosmic egg is found in several mythologies, such as the Orphic mythology of ancient Greece, which emphasizes the image of a large egg from which all the beings of the universe sprouted, like chicks, including the very bases of it ÔÇô Heaven and Earth. An identical panorama presents us with the Chinese genesis myth. In this creation myth, we are given an image that appeals to a chaotic mixture, in which the universe is immersed in total darkness. Afterwards, we witness the sacrifice of the titan, who manages to separate and constitute the two basic forces, representatives of the masculine principle Yang (Úś│), Heaven, and the feminine principle Yin (Úś┤), the earth. All of creation is an act of sacrifice of the portentous, which begins in the thousands of years in which the divine force separates the elements and culminates in the exhaustion of the titan. It is from Pangu's body and, through transformation, that all living beings, celestial and telluric, emerge. There is an immanent, living and animated energy that keeps a universe connected, as if in one body. The generous sacrifices donÔÇÖt stop there. Neither in China, nor in ancient Greece, do they become very clear when one thinks about the descriptions found for the creation of humanity by the Goddess N¤őwa (ňą│ňĘ▓), that in some version of the myth ÔÇťN¤őwa creates human beingsÔÇŁ (ňą│ňĘ▓ÚÇáń║║NăÜ w─ü z├áo r├ęn) after creating humanity with the help of the earth and filling it with people, she falls deeply asleep, such was the state of tiredness and one is left without knowing if he will not have woken up in paradise never to return to the world. The Goddess, after creating the animals, continued to feel alone, so "on the seventh day, she arrived at a lake. She took some clay and mixed it with water. When she saw her reflected image, she made a piece of clay similar to her" (Wang, Alves, 2009, 16). To make it move, it was enough to place the doll and all the others that would follow, in contact with the earth. The energy of this animated them, being in itself indispensable in the transition from dolls to the creation of humanity. As happened in the ancient Greek world, the cosmos arises from the void and primordial Chaos, then, through spontaneous generation, as stated in Hesiod's theogony, Mother-Earth Gaia, Eros, the God of Love, manifests itself and from this all the deified elements: the Sky or Uranus, the Sea and the Mountains, etc. Gaia, with the help of Uranus, generates the remaining children, some of them colossal and deformed creatures like the Cyclops, with fifty heads and one hundred arms, in addition to the titans, the giants, as well as the remaining gods, which Cronus, the god of time called titans, which meant in his idea ÔÇťarrogant and ambitious creaturesÔÇŁ. The Greek Gods had all human defects and virtues, they were evil, jealous, vengeful, but also kind and giving, depending on the way they behaved on their moods. In ancient Greece, humanity was not forged in the likeness of the gods, but, on the contrary, the gods were conceived in the image and likeness of humanity. Poor those divine forces that out of love for humanity tried to deceive their peers, as happened with the titan Prometheus who went to Olympus to steal fire from the gods to provide humanity with a powerful weapon. The episode took place after Epimetheus, Prometheus' younger brother, whose name means ÔÇťwhat he thinks afterÔÇŁ, endowed the animals, when he created them with the best virtues that he took from the saddlebag prepared by the younger gods: claws, fangs, wings, tentacles, beaks, stingers, speed and everything else that animals have. Prometheus, whose name means ÔÇťforesighted thinkerÔÇŁ, upon concluding that there was nothing left for himself on earth that would distinguish humanity, went to Olympus, deceiving and extorting the fire of the gods, thus endowing human beings with intelligence so that they could defend themselves from animals with physical capabilities far superior to theirs. To do so, he had to catch Hestia, the virgin goddess of the home, distracted, but the same did not happen to Zeus, the supreme leader of the gods, who was not fooled. He punished what he considered to be the supreme arrogance of the titan thief with a terrible torture. He shackled Prometheus in the Caucasus Mountains and ordered Hephaestus, god of forging and other arts, to make shackles to chain him in his furnace. The creator of humanity was trapped by Power and Force on a cliff, with arms and legs stretched out, held by the wrists and ankles, being visited daily by a voracious eagle, which tore apart his diaphragm and ate his liver. Aeschylus (525- 456 BC), the great Greek playwright, recounts the torture, inspired by Hesiod's Theogony, in the tragedy Prometheus Bound, leaving the hero to unburden himself at the end of the play that recounts his adventure in the gift of fire to Humanity: (ÔÇŽ) HereÔÇÖs the gust who, to bring terror upon me, It was sent by Zeus. Oh Majesty! Oh my mother! Divine ether! ÔÇĽ That, across the world beyond, phases roll wonderful hymn: the Light ÔÇô supreme good, to illuminate the tragic fate from everyone! See how great my misfortune is, The pain that tortures me - Unfairly!ÔÇŽ (Aeschylus, 1946, 115) ÔÇâPrometheus' sacrifice will have an echo in the Christian world, in which the son of God, Jesus Christ, also sacrifices himself for the love of Humanity. He dies on the cross to save men, who invariably behave badly. But there is a big difference in the way Chinese and Christians view the cosmos, with ancient Greece establishing the bridge between both worldviews. From the Christian perspective, God created the world out of nothing (Creatio ex nihilo). It is a transcendent, personal creation with the artist's signature. The fruit of creation may not have been the best, but it will always remain unexplained how evil entered the world, even with Saint Augustine defending the theory of human free will. ÔÇâHere is the account of creation ex nihilo in the book of Genesis of the Old Testament (Gen 1): In the beginning God created Heaven and Earth. The Earth was formless and empty; darkness covered the abyss and a fierce wind blew over the waters. God said: ÔÇťLet there be light!ÔÇŁ And the light began to exist. God saw that the light was good. God separated the light from the darkness: the light God called ÔÇťdayÔÇŁ and the darkness he called ÔÇťnightÔÇŁ. There was an afternoon and a morning, it was the first day. Then followed the creation of the firmament, which God called heaven on the second day. On the third day, he created the earth, the sea and the plants. On the fourth day the sun, moon and stars were created. On the fifth day, the fish, the birds and all living beings. On the sixth day, animals were divided into species and man was created. On the seventh day he rested. Regarding the creation of humanity, God in one version creates them directly and in his image and likeness (Gn 1, 27-31), in the other he creates first the man and then the woman from his rib (Gn 2,18-23). After which, a long road is covered until we reach the New Testament and the sacrifice of Jesus Christ, who died on the cross for the love of humanity. There is a big difference, from a philosophical point of view, between the Chinese and Greek immanentist cosmologies and the Christian transcendent one. The first obey the principle of transformation of elements that have always been immersed in primordial chaos and, therefore, present an interrelated cosmological image, in which the elements or ten thousand beings have root affinities that allow them to communicate and transmute themselves whenever necessary. Greek gods, for example, take whatever forms they wish, just like Chinese deities and immortals. In the Christian West, the absence of a cosmic egg or primordial chaos hinders and prevents transmutation. Beings are all separate and in an external position in relation to divinity, since if one is made in the image of god, one is not the divine himself and to achieve this status a mystical union with him is supposed. The profane exterior, split, distant will have to travel a long path, which perhaps involves martyrdom and sacrifice already experienced by the son of God. In any case, even within the scope of immanentist philosophies, guided by the principle of transformation, sacrifice is also always present, when combined with love for humanity, as in the case of Prometheus or, in part, of N¤őwa; or without love, but by natural essence, when the alliance is made over time, if the example of Pangu is considered. The gods are sacrificed, but they are not alone. The sacrifice is universal, because natural creatures are also martyred for love or time in the three cosmogonies presented, they immolate themselves to deities, some more distant, others so close that they can only be intuited from within. References Holy Bible. 1997. S├úo Paulo: Paulus. Squirrel. 1946. Prometheus Fettered. Translated by Eduardo Scarlatti. Lisbon: Livraria Luso-Espanhola, Lda ÔÇťŠÁĹňĄęŔ»┤ÔÇŁ (Hun Tian theory) In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink, accessed February 25, 2026. ÔÇťšŤśňĆĄň╝ÇňĄęÔÇŁ (Pangu separates Heaven). In Baidu. Baike. https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E7%9B%98%E5%8F%A4%E5%BC%80%E5%A4%A9/74007´╝î2008ň╣┤6Šťł7ŠŚą, accessed February 24, 2026. Johnston, Sarah Iles. 2025. Gods and Mortals. Odivelas: Soul of Books Martins, Jo├úo Cancelo. 2023. The Universal Grammar of Myth: Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Myths of Origin. Lisbon: Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, University of Macau. Wang Suoying, Ana Cristina Alves. 2009. Myths and Legends of the land of the Dragon. Lisbon: Path. Notes: I This space has the collaboration of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Center, in Lisbon, and the opinions expressed in the article are the sole responsibility of the authors. According to scholar Jo├úo Martins in The Universal Grammar of Myth. Comparative and Contrastive Analysis of Chinese Origin Myths (2023, 143, note 100) The Three Five Calendar concerns the now lost historical records of the Three Sovereign Deities and the Five Gods. iii The Hun Tian theory, founded by Zhang Heng (ň╝áŔíí), an astronomer from the Han Dynasty, is an astronomical theory between the geocentric and heliocentric theses, in which it is argued that the universe is infinite, the movement of celestial bodies is regular, moonlight is the reflection of sunlight and lunar eclipses are caused by the Earth blocking sunlight. To prove his Hun Tian Theory, Zhang Heng built the first ÔÇťwater-powered armillary sphereÔÇŁ, ushering in an era of more accurate astronomical observation. He then used more rigorous astronomical observation data to corroborate his theory in explaining various celestial phenomena, influencing astronomy for more than 1500 years. (cf https://baike.baidu.com/item/%E6%B5%91%E5%A4%A9%E8%AF%B4/717090?fromModule=lemma_inlink)

item.title: Paulo Maia and Carmo
item.description: Shen Deqian (1673-1769), whom Emperor Qianlong (r. 1736-95) called the "illustrious old scholar of Jiangnan", was a notable and influential high official, poet, essayist and master of thought with a career marked by successive surprises, such as the unusual fact that he only passed the difficult jinshi exam in 1739, after seventeen attempts and when he had already sixty-seven years. But soon after, he would assume important roles as deputy minister in the Ministry of Rites or Grand Tutor of the Royal Prince. A role that he had long performed for private individuals. The recognition that Qianlong showed him included not only the ┬źimperial favor┬╗ that allowed him to stroll in the imperial gardens but also the rare privilege of having a temple erected in his name for which the emperor wrote with his brush the phrase: ┬źVenerable dean of the world of poetry┬╗. Many decrees and memorials were written with the authority that possessed his brush, such as the one that states that in 1699, ÔÇťEmperor Kangxi declared that from now on, Spring Dawn on the Su (Shi) Embankment will be the first name on the list of the Ten Views of the West Lake.ÔÇŁ At the time of his death, the emperor himself composed an elegy for him. No one would deny the master's persistent and valiant exemplary career. But suddenly in 1778, he had already been dead for eight years, his honorary titles were taken away, his commemorative temple was abolished, his posthumous name was revoked, his tombstone was torn down, his body was even exhumed and quartered, among other punishments. And all because of the reference to a flower. During a period of literary inquisitions, a subtle reference to the perenniality of the previous dynasty was discovered in a work by Xu Shukui (1703-1763). That Shen Deqian, his biographer and fellow countryman from Jiangsu, where there was unstoppable resistance to Manchu power, praised and quoted in the verses: ┬źNothing but the color red can be the true one,/ Even if foreign varieties proclaim themselves ┬źqueen of flowers┬╗ (huawang, a popular name for peony). Both were severely condemned, even post-mortem. Wang Jiu (1745-98), a painter who Wang Yu included in his Oriental Estate Painting Manual at the end of the 18th century, Dongzhuang hualun, as one of the Four Little Wangs, recalled verses by Shen Deqian in the year before his conviction, in a small album (ink on paper, 13.8 x 20.5 cm, for sale at Christie's). On one of the pages there is just a willow tree, an undulating emblem of longing, and beneath it a fisherman in a boat evoking the desire to sail free over the danger of the waters. Which sometimes came only in an allusion to red peonies, and could even have a brutal punishment, but if one was skillful, as Shen Deqian wrote about Meng Haoran's poetry, ┬źthe language will be simple, but the flavor will remain.┬╗

item.title: Today Macau
item.description:
By Manuel Silv├ęrio ÔÇô former president of the Sports Institute and co-founder of the Macau Olympic Committee ┬á The written question from Deputy Leong Sun Iok raises a timely and, above all, concrete point: how to transform recent experiences ÔÇö in particular the cycling event that linked Macau, Hong Kong and Guangdong ÔÇö into institutionalized advantages, capable of reinforcing regional cooperation and consolidating Macau as a Sports City. The merit of this position also lies in the profile of those who place it. Leong Sun Iok is a young deputy, close to the field, who regularly accompanies athletes and practitioners. And it is not insignificant that it is linked to the Federation of Workers' Associations of Macau (FAOM), an institution with strong social and sporting traditions, where many young people ÔÇö and also ÔÇťanonymousÔÇŁ practitioners ÔÇö have found, over decades, space to train, compete and create active lifestyle habits. Just remember the emblematic Campo dos Oper├írios, in the heart of the city, next to the Portuguese School, where the Grand Lisboa stands today ÔÇö a symbol of how Macau has changed, but where the sporting memory remains. The central issue is simple: pioneering experiences are important, but what counts is what happens afterwards. And this is where the deputy's question is decisive: when introducing or hosting international events in the future, will there be conditions to take advantage of the experience gained in the National Games? Will there be a mechanism to facilitate authorizations and coordination with Guangdong and Hong Kong? ┬á What the Government might do ÔÇťnextÔÇŁ (realistic forecast) Without guessing too much, there are a set of steps that the Government could realistically take to transform these experiences into a permanent capacity:
ÔüáCreate an interdepartmental working group (security, traffic, tourism, sport, border issues and health), with regular meetings and a clear decision-making method. Establish a ÔÇťgreen channelÔÇŁ for ÔÇťthree-regionÔÇŁ events, with defined criteria, fixed deadlines and a predictable administrative circuit. ÔüáConsolidate an annual or multi-annual calendar with 1ÔÇô2 anchor events (e.g. a cross-border cycling race and a half marathon/border crossing race). Strengthen cooperation with Guangdong and Hong Kong in a technical format, not just political: operational protocols, contingency plans and coordination between teams on the ground. ÔüáSecure partners with operational capacity and sponsorship, including robust private sector participation, where this increases efficiency and scale.
The risk is known: everything will be left with ÔÇťgood intentionsÔÇŁ if there is no owner of the process ÔÇö a leading entity ÔÇö and execution goals with accountability. If Macau really wants to gain scale and regional relevance through sport, the path passes less through announcements and more through mechanisms, deadlines, leadership and execution. Deputy Leong Sun Iok's interpellation, in this sense, is a good starting point to ask the right question ÔÇö and, above all, to demand operational answers.

item.title: Jos├ę Sim├Áes Morais
item.description: The Jesuit Matteo Ricci (1552-1610) initially interpreted Chinese rites as superstitious, but to understand them better he studied the doctrine of Confucius, on which Chinese society and its political system were based with the principles of Order, Peace and Harmony. The name Confucius was given by Ricci to Kong Fu Zi ´╝łňşöňĄźňşÉ´╝î 551-479 BC), master Kong (Kongzi), who was born in the Spring-Autumn Period (770-476 BC), two thousand years ago. He lived during the Eastern Zhou Dynasty, when hundreds of small kingdoms that had previously been federated began to be conquered and great instability arose due to continuous wars between the increasingly larger and stronger kingdoms. Without qualified rulers for this new space of strategic management, many enlightened masters appeared, including Kongzi, who offered their knowledge, placing them at the service of the king for governance. Kongzi reorganized ancient knowledge and, being a great educator, transmitted it to his disciples. He instilled respect for the Ancestors, as a way of life he proposed the improvement of ethics through righteousness, benevolence and virtues. As a philosophy, the path of practical wisdom for harmony in social life, education and politics, attaching great importance to Peace, Harmony and Order as a principle of good governance and organization of the State. After his death in 479 BC, the teachings, despite having been marginalized at the time, had Confucian followers, excellent and powerful administrators. In 213 B.C.E. Most of the writings were destroyed by fire, but reproduced from memory they were perpetuated in Chinese society from the Han dynasty onwards (206 BC-220). Kongzi advocated the concept of Harmony: an ordered Universe, in which human beings must seek to maintain harmonious relationships with Nature, with themselves and with others. He regulated human relationships, for which he found five fundamental ones: sovereign and subjects, parents and children, husband and wife, older brother and younger brothers and between friends. "If the duties between one another are observed, there is harmony; otherwise, disharmony reigns. The most important relationship is that of father and son and the central virtue that governs it is filial piety (ňşŁ, xiao). On this virtue and those that accompany it ÔÇô obedience, respect and service ÔÇô the structure of Chinese society was built." Between quotation marks is what I believe to be by Father Benjamim Videira Pires, but it could have another author and for this mistake I apologize and thank anyone who corrects and dispels this doubt. The article from here onwards is structured around this text, from which the citations come, as I believe the subject deserves enough importance to be published. The kow-tow in rites "Filial piety should not cease with the death of parents; the child must continue to serve them as if they were alive, and this duty extends to all ancestors of the family. Confucius, by extending filial piety to the dead, fostered this virtue in life", taking actions to be carried out as a show of respect for ancestors and the desire to achieve their perfections with the help of example, imitating them; ÔÇô the best way to honor family. In memory of the Ancestors, rituals were carried out in the houses where wooden tablets were found inscribed with the names of the deceased male ancestors, father, grandfather and great-grandfather, as when the great-great-grandfather became the tablet the right to appear in the Family Temple, until after a few generations it entered that of the Ancestors. Expressions of reverence and respect were addressed to the tablets, in an environment with flowers, candles, incense, as the intention is ÔÇťto arrange a certain place or term to which they can elevate their minds; and to which, as to a painting, they can direct the customary honors, as if they were present.ÔÇŁ In this ceremony, "kau-t├íu (headbanging) was performed, in which a person kneels and bows deeply until their forehead touches the ground. It is the Chinese custom of respect: nine prostrations to the throne at imperial receptions; prostrations in the courts before magistrates and of children before their parents and in-laws. Thus, there is nothing religious about the prostrations at funerals before the coffin and it could not be said that it was worship of the dead." All this led Ricci to modify the original opinion: "what to the European seemed like superstition and worship, to the Chinese it was just a symbolic expression of filial piety. The fact that they used the same ceremonies for the living led us to believe that they were not religious acts when applied to the dead. Such was the case with the burning of incense (ÔÇŽ). Educated people used to welcome guests with incense, which was part of the ceremonial etiquette, which gave grace and harmony to social relations, being a sign of respect. There was therefore no reason to give it a religious meaning when it was used at funerals, before tablets or in the rooms of Confucius.ÔÇŁ After long discussions with Chinese friends, Ricci concluded that there was nothing idolatrous in the ancestral rites or perhaps superstitious and "submitted these opinions to Valignano, who discussed them with his consultants. The conclusion was that it was permitted to participate in the solemn rites in honor of the family's ancestors on the condition that paper money was not burned, but it was not permitted to take part in the solemn ceremonies in honor of Confucius with the sacrifice of a victim, because, as he said Furtado, ÔÇťsmell strongly of superstitionÔÇŁ (Quoted by Dunne, Generation of Giants).ÔÇŁ On days prescribed by the ritual, with the appearance of religious sacrifices, an animal (immolated on site), wine, silkÔÇŽ was offered to the ancestors, following a family banquet. According to the Jesuits, ÔÇťThe idea of ÔÇőÔÇőspirits taking nourishment from the food offered should be repudiated; and of not making prayers or requests to the deadÔÇŁ. Other ceremonies the Jesuits permitted and even observed. ÔÇťOn the occasion of someone's death, the friends, who came to offer their condolences, would wear, in mourning, a white silk robe; upon entering the room, they would perform the kau-t'au four times in front of the deceased; the deceased's children would then repeat the same gesture; the visitor, approaching closer to the coffin, would perform the same ceremony again. The rites in honor of Confucius were only practiced by literate people and did not differ in essence from ancestral ceremonies,ÔÇŁ and after ÔÇťpassing the exams, prostrations were made before ConfuciusÔÇÖ tablet in a room dedicated to him. The same rites, prostrations and burning of incense were observed by officials and scholars on the days of the new moon and full moon.ÔÇŁ Confucianism is based on the teachings of Kong Fuzi, a reformer who, using the rites of the Zhou, reorganized the order of society and structured human thought in China through the concepts of virtue, justice, tolerance and generosity. For practical wisdom, the importance of Astronomy and the preparation of the Calendar, as any inaccuracy disturbs the balance between Heaven and Earth. ň«î

item.title: Paulo Maia and Carmo
item.description: Shen Deqian (1673-1769), whom Emperor Qianlong (r. 1736-95) called the "illustrious old scholar of Jiangnan", was a notable and influential high official, poet, essayist and master of thought with a career marked by successive surprises, such as the unusual fact that he only passed the difficult jinshi exam in 1739, after seventeen attempts and when he had already sixty-seven years. But soon after, he would assume important roles as deputy minister in the Ministry of Rites or Grand Tutor of the Royal Prince. A role that he had long performed for private individuals. The recognition that Qianlong showed him included not only the ┬źimperial favor┬╗ that allowed him to stroll in the imperial gardens but also the rare privilege of having a temple erected in his name for which the emperor wrote with his brush the phrase: ┬źVenerable dean of the world of poetry┬╗. Many decrees and memorials were written with the authority that possessed his brush, such as the one that states that in 1699, ÔÇťEmperor Kangxi declared that from now on, Spring Dawn on the Su (Shi) Embankment will be the first name on the list of the Ten Views of the West Lake.ÔÇŁ At the time of his death, the emperor himself composed an elegy for him. No one would deny the master's persistent and valiant exemplary career. But suddenly in 1778, he had already been dead for eight years, his honorary titles were taken away, his commemorative temple was abolished, his posthumous name was revoked, his tombstone was torn down, his body was even exhumed and quartered, among other punishments. And all because of the reference to a flower. During a period of literary inquisitions, a subtle reference to the perenniality of the previous dynasty was discovered in a work by Xu Shukui (1703-1763). That Shen Deqian, his biographer and fellow countryman from Jiangsu, where there was unstoppable resistance to Manchu power, praised and quoted in the verses: ┬źNothing but the color red can be the true one,/ Even if foreign varieties proclaim themselves ┬źqueen of flowers┬╗ (huawang, a popular name for peony). Both were severely condemned, even post-mortem. Wang Jiu (1745-98), a painter who Wang Yu included in his Oriental Estate Painting Manual at the end of the 18th century, Dongzhuang hualun, as one of the Four Little Wangs, recalled verses by Shen Deqian in the year before his conviction, in a small album (ink on paper, 13.8 x 20.5 cm, for sale at Christie's). On one of the pages there is just a willow tree, an undulating emblem of longing, and beneath it a fisherman in a boat evoking the desire to sail free over the danger of the waters. Which sometimes came only in an allusion to red peonies, and could even have a brutal punishment, but if one was skillful, as Shen Deqian wrote about Meng Haoran's poetry, ┬źthe language will be simple, but the flavor will remain.┬╗

item.title: Iran | War breaks out in the Middle East with attacks that killed Khamenei
item.description:
The United States and Israel launched a series of attacks against Iran. One of the fatal victims was Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as well as several Iranian military leaders. The Shah's heir, Reza Pahlavi, believes in a bright future, at a time when Tehran's counterattacks are spreading across the Middle East
Israel and the United States of America (USA) joined in a joint operation to overthrow the Iranian regime, at a time when, in the country, there were already some demonstrations against the authorities. On Saturday, a series of bombings were launched against Iran, with the declared aim of forcing a change of regime. The country responded with attacks on Israel and US military bases, and the death of Iran's supreme religious leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who had been in power for 36 years, was also confirmed. According to Lusa, the announcement was made on Iranian state television at 5am local time, with a reporter crying over what had happened. Khameinei was 86 years old and, for Trump, his death now offers the Iranian population a ÔÇťgreater chanceÔÇŁ of ÔÇťrecoveringÔÇŁ the country. The Iranian regime declared 40 days of mourning for the death of the religious leader. In addition to Khamenei, the chief of staff of the Iranian Armed Forces, Abdolrahim Mousavi, was also killed, along with other high-ranking generals during the US and Israeli attacks against the country, Iranian state television reported yesterday. Meanwhile, China expressed ÔÇťgreat concernÔÇŁ about the situation in the Middle East and called for respect for Iran's sovereignty and territorial integrity. ÔÇťIran's sovereignty, security and territorial integrity must be respected,ÔÇŁ said the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a statement cited by the North American news agency The Associated Press (AP). Beijing called for ÔÇťan immediate cessation of military actions and that there is no further escalation of a tense situationÔÇŁ. He also called for the ÔÇťresumption of dialogue and negotiationÔÇŁ and efforts to ÔÇťmaintain peace and stability in the Middle EastÔÇŁ, according to the statement also cited by the Spanish news agency Europa Press (EP). China has recommended the departure of its citizens from Iran, advising its citizens not to travel to the region, due to the ÔÇťmarked increase in security risksÔÇŁ, as the country faces repeated threats of attacks from the US. ÔÇťChinese citizens currently in Iran should reinforce their security precautions and leave the country as soon as possible,ÔÇŁ the Consular Affairs Department of the Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs said in a statement. A nuclear issue North American President Donald Trump has threatened to attack Iran due to the authorities' repression of protests against the regime and alleging that Tehran is developing nuclear weapons, despite having attacked sites on Iranian soil in June precisely with the aim of stopping the alleged nuclear program. At the end of the campaign, Trump claimed that Iran's nuclear program had been completely obliterated. On Tuesday - and despite talks in Geneva about an agreement regarding Iran's nuclear program - Trump once again said, during his State of the Nation address in Congress, that he will not allow Iran to have nuclear weapons. In 2015, Iran signed an agreement to limit its nuclear development ÔÇô ÔÇőÔÇőin exchange for the relief of economic sanctions on the country ÔÇô with the five permanent members of the UN Security Council ÔÇö the United States, Russia, China, France and the United Kingdom, as well as with Germany and the European Union. However, the agreement lost effect after Trump himself unilaterally withdrew the United States from the treaty in 2018, during his first term (2017-2021). China's recommendations join those of several other countries that have raised their security alerts, such as Sweden, Serbia, Germany, India, South Korea, Brazil, Australia, the United Kingdom or Canada. Tension in the Middle East Ali Khamenei's death puts the future of the Islamic Republic in doubt and increases the risk of regional instability. ÔÇťKhamenei, one of the most evil people in history, is dead,ÔÇŁ Trump wrote in a social media post, warning of the continuation of ÔÇťheavy and precise bombingsÔÇŁ throughout the week and even beyond. Saturday's attack opened a new chapter in the US intervention in Iran, bringing with it the potential for retaliatory violence and a broader war. The truth is that there are already pockets of tension thanks to the Iranian response, with attacks on around ten countries, including the United Arab Emirates. In Dubai, a strong explosion occurred in a luxury hotel area, causing four injuries, while Dubai International Airport was also the target of an attack, leading to the cancellation of all flights. Another source of tension occurred in Karachi, Pakistan, resulting in the death of six people and 20 injuries during a protest near the fortified area of ÔÇőÔÇőthe United States Consulate. According to local police spokesman Rehan Ali, to CNN, ÔÇťhundreds of people suddenly appeared in the vicinityÔÇŁ of the diplomatic mission, leading to a rapid mobilization of security forces. The screams captured on the recordings suggest that the protest was motivated by attacks involving the United States and Israel against neighboring Iran. A country without a leader Khamenei's death in the Trump Administration's second attack on Iran in eight months creates a leadership vacuum, given the absence of a known ayatollah successor, and also because the 86-year-old supreme leader had the final say on all major policies during decades in power. Khamenei led Iran's clerical establishment and its paramilitary Revolutionary Guard, the two main centers of power in the ruling theocracy. Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran's National Security Council, said on Saturday that Israel and the United States "will regret their actions." ÔÇťThe brave soldiers and the great nation of Iran will teach an unforgettable lesson to the infernal international oppressors,ÔÇŁ Larijani posted on the X social network. The joint operation by the United States and Israel, which according to authorities was planned for months, took place this Saturday, during the Muslim holy month of Ramadan and at the beginning of the Iranian work week, and followed negotiations and warnings from Trump, who last year trumpeted his government's success in crippling the country's nuclear program. About 12 hours after the attacks began, the U.S. military reported that there were no American casualties and that damage to U.S. bases was minimal despite ÔÇťhundreds of Iranian missile and drone strikes.ÔÇŁ According to US forces, targets in Iran included Revolutionary Guard command facilities, air defense systems, missile and drone launch sites and military airfields. Regarding Khamenei's death, Trump declared that the ayatollah ÔÇťwas unable to escape highly sophisticated intelligence and tracking systemsÔÇŁ. ÔÇťWorking closely with Israel, there was nothing he, or the other leaders who were killed with him, could do,ÔÇŁ said the American President. ÔÇťThis is the greatest opportunity for the Iranian people to take back their country,ÔÇŁ he said. An Iranian diplomat told the United Nations Security Council that hundreds of civilians were killed and injured in the attacks. Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at Israel and US military bases in the region, and fighting continued into the night. US Democrats criticized the fact that Trump took measures without authorization from Congress, but White House spokeswoman Karoline Leavitt said that the Administration informed several Republican and Democratic leaders in Congress in advance. What does the Shah's son say At a time when regime change appears to be imminent in Iran, but with no clear understanding of how it will happen, one of the voices that has defended US and Israeli action is Reza Pahlavi, son of the former Shah. Having lived in the USA for decades, since the Iranian revolution of 1979, Pahlavi is now arguing that it is time to take to the streets. According to Lusa, the heir stated, in an editorial published in The Washington Post, that he intends to take on a role in the transition process in Iran. "Many Iranians, often despite bullets, asked me to lead this transition. I am impressed by your courage and responded to your call", he wrote. "Our path forward will be transparent: a new Constitution written and ratified by referendum, followed by free elections under international supervision. When Iranians vote, the transitional government will be dissolved." For the past 47 years, Pahlavi has been a critical voice of the Iranian regime, now siding with Trump and Israel. Domestically and regionally, Pahlavi is widely regarded as a puppet of Washington and Tel Aviv. In the article, he praises the entire trajectory that Trump has taken towards Iran, saying that the country's ÔÇťrebirthÔÇŁ ÔÇťwill mark the beginning of an era of peace and prosperityÔÇŁ. Pahlavi also highlighted that ÔÇťIran is not IraqÔÇŁ. "We will not repeat the mistakes that followed this [Iraq] conflict. There will be no dissolution of institutions, no power vacuum, no chaos", he said, recalling that since last year he has been defending the so-called "Iran Prosperity Project". It is a national political and economic plan to establish a transitional provisional government after the fall of the ayatollahs. The Iranian activist guarantees that ÔÇťthis detailed roadmap for national recoveryÔÇŁ includes a plan for ÔÇťthe first 100 days after the collapse of the regime and the long-term reconstruction and stabilizationÔÇŁ of the country and ÔÇťhas the support of numerous business leaders from around the worldÔÇŁ. ÔÇťA democratic Iran would transform the Middle East, converting one of the most persistent sources of unrest in the world into a pillar of regional stabilityÔÇŁ, says Pahlavi, who praises the ÔÇťhistoricÔÇŁ Abraham agreements, promoted by Trump in his first term.

item.title: Crime | Study reveals perception of increase, even with downward trend
item.description: Although official data reveals fewer crimes in the Macao SAR, this reality is not reflected in society's perception. A study reveals that in Macau only 13 percent of respondents were aware of the drop in crime and that more than 35 percent believed that crime had increased The study ÔÇťExamining fear of crime among Macao residents: Vulnerability, victimization and situational factorsÔÇŁ, published in the journal International Review of Victimology, reveals that in the territory the population does not fear crime, although perceptions regarding crime are not always in line with official data. The work, authored by Yixuan Wang, Donna Soi Wan Leong and Lichao Lu, from the University of Macau (UM) and Jianhong Liu, from the Macau University of Science and Technology (MUST), reveals that there was, between 2021 and 2022, a ÔÇť13.9 percent decline in crimeÔÇŁ, according to data released by the Security Services Directorate in 2023. However, ÔÇťthe survey data from this study reveals a significant discrepancy in perception: only 12.83 percent of respondents reported being aware of the decline, while 35.72 percent believed that crime had increased,ÔÇŁ it reads. Furthermore, ÔÇťthe vast majority of respondents did not demonstrate emotional fearÔÇŁ in the face of crime, as ÔÇť78.87 percent reported zero unsafe places and only 2.77 percent identified two or moreÔÇŁ unsafe areas in Macau. Meanwhile, ÔÇťonly 9.69 percent of respondents reported feeling at risk of victimization, indicating that the vast majority did not feel at riskÔÇŁ. The study also shows that ÔÇťthe proportion of residents who reported defensive behavioral responses was the lowest among the fear dimensions, with only 2.89 percent of respondents indicating that they had adopted defensive behaviors in the previous 12 monthsÔÇŁ. The authors describe how Macau constitutes a paradigmatic example for analyzing social fear of crime and perceptions regarding official data, as it is a special administrative region that ÔÇťfunctions under the ÔÇśone country, two systemsÔÇÖ governance framework, characterized by a distinct sociopolitical and legal structure, along with a multicultural populationÔÇŁ. Furthermore, it is mentioned that Macau has an economy ÔÇťthat depends exclusively on gambling and tourism, which drives rapid financial circulation and large-scale population mobilityÔÇŁ, being a ÔÇťsmall urban territory with an exceptionally high population densityÔÇŁ. In this way, the particular characteristics of the territory ÔÇťraise questions about the applicability of existing models of fear of crime, since residents' perceptions of crime and disorder can be shaped by dense urban environments and rapid population flowsÔÇŁ. A question of age The study was carried out based on the 2022 Macau Victimization Survey, managed by the Macau Society of Criminology, a project funded by the Macau Foundation. The target population of the survey were residents over 18 years of age, and for this study, additional telephone interviews were carried out with 1,102 people, resulting in a final sample of 795 people. This study also states that ÔÇťthe results reveal consistencies and deviations in relation to previous researchÔÇŁ, as ÔÇťcontrary to what has been established, age and gender do not significantly influence the fear of crimeÔÇŁ. For the authors, the ÔÇťunexpected resultÔÇŁ can be explained by ÔÇťinstitutional and Macau-specific factorsÔÇŁ, as the ÔÇťMacau Government has placed great emphasis on investment in social well-being, which can act as a buffer against fear for potentially vulnerable groupsÔÇŁ. The measure of financial support for the repair of buildings is highlighted, such as the Building Maintenance Support Scheme and the Subsidy Scheme for Maintenance of Common Parts, which ÔÇťfurther reduces the need for residents to implement additional defensive strategies at the domestic levelÔÇŁ. The study highlights the ÔÇťextensive welfare infrastructure in MacauÔÇŁ, such as ÔÇťuniversal healthcare, old-age pensions and social protection schemes, which can mitigate perceived vulnerability among the elderlyÔÇŁ. Laws that help Regarding gender-based violence, it is described how the Domestic Violence Law and ÔÇťstrengthened support systems for victims can contribute to a feeling of security associated with genderÔÇŁ. In 2022, there were 22 cases of rape and eight of sexual coercion, according to data from the Institute of Social Action (IAS) cited in the study. The authors also highlight that Macau's Gender Inequality Index was 0.06 in 2021, ÔÇťsubstantially lowerÔÇŁ than that of mainland China (0.192), Japan (0.083) and the global average (0.457). This indicates ÔÇťa relatively equitable environment in terms of genderÔÇŁ, according to mentions made by the IAS in 2021 and the United Nations Development Program, in 2022. The academics describe how ÔÇťthese factors, taken together, may mitigate the relevance of physical vulnerability in the formation of fear of crime, explaining the lack of significant effects of age and genderÔÇŁ. The study highlights that the fact that ÔÇťconsistently low levels of fearÔÇŁ regarding crime can be ÔÇťpartially attributed to the Macau Government's substantial investment in public security and the high density of police resourcesÔÇŁ. It appears, therefore, that ÔÇťthe strong policing and investment in public security in MacauÔÇŁ, even with the low crime rate, results ÔÇťin an equally limited prevalence of victimization in the sampleÔÇŁ. Furthermore, "the relatively low levels of fear reported by Macau residents can be explained by both substantive cultural mechanisms and methodological factors. At the substantive level, collectivist and relational cultural orientations can exert a genuinely buffering effect", since in Chinese societies "individuals are embedded in dense family, kinship and community networks", which can facilitate "access to social support and mitigate stress and fear responses", the study reads.
