item.description: A new exhibition opens this Wednesday at the Vila da Taipa Cultural Association, ÔÇťShow-Off 4.0ÔÇŁ, which showcases the art collections of Macau residents and personalities. Continuing a series of exhibitions already held, this time the works acquired by jurist Lu├şs Pessanha, artist Chao Iok Leng and economist Jos├ę Duarte are revealed. The Vila da Taipa Cultural Association, located in the heart of this picturesque area of ÔÇőÔÇőMacau, is preparing to host another exhibition based on the collecting of works of art, providing a view of different artistic perspectives. ÔÇťShow-Off 4.0ÔÇŁ presents the works that figures based in Macau, or natives of the territory, have acquired and have collected at home, showing the collections of jurist Lu├şs Pessanha, economist Jos├ę Isaac Duarte and artist Chao Iok Leng to the public. The exhibition will be on display until May 15th. According to a statement from the association, this exhibition is a ÔÇťtrue artistic treasure, bringing together works acquired by three renowned collectors with paths marked by diversityÔÇŁ, and you can see ÔÇťworks from both the local and international contextÔÇŁ. ÔÇťShow-Off 4.0ÔÇŁ reveals ÔÇťideas related to art collecting and aims to promote the act of collecting as a cultural asset driven by individual initiativeÔÇŁ, and can also ÔÇťexpand the sense of common heritageÔÇŁ or even ÔÇťfoster the vitality of society and add diversity to local economies, including through activities such as art tradingÔÇŁ, without forgetting the possibility of ÔÇťsupporting local and international talentÔÇŁ. Personality question The organizers of this exhibition say that ÔÇťeach collection presented reveals personality traits of its collectorÔÇŁ, and in the case of Jos├ę Duarte, with a long history linked to art and organizing exhibitions, this characteristic is very evident. It should be noted that Jos├ę Duarte was the founder and managing partner of Galeria Amagao, located in the Artyzen Grand Lapa Macau hotel. In this way, Jos├ę Duarte brought to ÔÇťShow-Off 4.0ÔÇŁ works by Malangatana Valente Ngwenya, one of the most important Mozambican artists, having also been a poet, muralist, cultural activist and political figure. Lu├şs Pessanha, on the other hand, has family connections to art, as his mother is the painter Ana Maria Pessanha. According to the same note, Lu├şs has been collecting works of art together with his partner, and the collection now fills the walls of his home. In the case of Chao Iok Leng, vice-president of the Yi Un Calligraphy and Painting Association of Macau, his journey linked to the arts has been a long one, as, in the 1980s, he founded the Ngai Chi Hin Bookstore in the territory. This space provided ÔÇťthe four treasures of the studioÔÇŁ, namely materials such as brushes, paint, paper and inkwell, as well as craft materials, ÔÇťbenefiting local artists and gaining wide recognition in the artistic communityÔÇŁ. In 2000 he created the Nam Fan Lou fan shop. In this exhibition, Chao presents two plaques with the names of his former establishments, along with personal objects that he kept at home with great pride and deep emotional connection. The signs, named zh─üop├íi, ÔÇťhave a long cultural tradition and were once a striking element of the Chinese urban landscapeÔÇŁ. Quoted in the same note, Jo├úo ├ô, architect and president of the association, described it as ÔÇťan honor to invite these three art collectors to present their various collections in Vila da Taipa, following the success of the experimental exhibitions held over the last three yearsÔÇŁ. It is a ÔÇťrare and special exhibitionÔÇŁ, which aims to ÔÇťexplore the nature of collecting and stimulate visually inspiring conversations around the cultural assets of individuals with very specific and deeply personal interestsÔÇŁ. he added. Entry to this exhibition is free.

item.title: Iran | Oil and closure of the Strait of Hormuz inflate Portuguese products
item.description: The consequences of the war in Iran will be felt in the prices of products imported from Europe, including Portugal. Maritime cargo transportation could take more than three months, whereas in the past just one month was enough. The Macau Suppliers Association also warns of the rise in oil prices and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz ┬á In addition to the blood shed in the Middle East, the offensive in the United States and Israel against Iran, and Tehran's response will have repercussions on the global economy, with Macau not being immune, despite the lessons learned during the pandemic. The president of the Macau Suppliers Union Association, Ip Sio Man, predicts that the prices of imported products will rise, with a particular impact on trade between Europe and Asia via maritime transport. Speaking to the newspaper Ou Mun, the representative stated that the war in the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to navigation will further delay maritime cargo transport that fuels commercial relations between Europe and Asia. The official indicates that before the tensions in the Red Sea, caused by the blockade of the Houthis in Yemen in response to the genocide in Gaza, the transport of cargo ships between Europe and Asia had already increased from one to two months. With the new offensive and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, transport could exceed three months. Among the products imported by Macau that could be most affected, Ip Sio Man highlighted Portuguese wine, olive oil and canned products. Containers and shelves The economic effect of the conflict will also be felt in the increase in the price of oil, which in turn will push up inflation in transport costs. The association leader points out that currently a cargo container costs 10 thousand patacas, but that this value will certainly rise, increased by fears of exchange rate volatility. All these constraints will be felt even before the products reach the shelves of supermarkets and commercial surfaces. Transport delays will force traders to expand their inventory and the storage cycle could go from two to three months to a period that could reach half a year. This disruption will result in increased storage costs. Given all these factors, Ip Sio Man concludes that an increase in the prices of imported products is inevitable. It is recalled that last year, the unit value index of exports rose 1.9 percent in annual terms, which was the highest increase in the last 12 years. The tariff war also comes into these accounts. Ip Sio Man claims that domestic production costs in the United States have increased with Donald Trump's tariffs, increasing pressure on manufacturing industries globally and making international trade and imports more expensive. However, the official highlights that European products represent a relatively small share of the Macau market, excluding luxury goods and dairy products (which occupy second place in terms of place of origin). In relation to this panorama, Ip Sio Man states that following the Covid-19 pandemic, many local suppliers were forced to decentralize their supply chains, looking for closer production sources. Therefore, despite maintaining European product sources, Macau suppliers began to purchase more dairy products in Australia, New Zealand and Mainland China, softening the impact of the European logistics chain.

item.title: Fuels | Portal shows prices with opposite trends
item.description: The information on the Consumer Council portal about vehicles shows prices that go months without being updated. However, the three vehicle fuels that allow comparisons to be made with the days before and during the war in Iran show variations to suit all tastes. ┬á Following the bombings of Iran by the United States and Israel, the price of oil has increased. However, the little information available on the Consumer Council (CC) portal about fuels shows a market with opposite variations. Despite making data on fuel prices available, the Consumer Council indicates that the update is carried out irregularly. Therefore, of the 11 fuels presented on the portal, it is only possible to compare three types, taking into account the price in the week before the bombings and in the days following. In the remaining cases, prices have not been updated for months or weeks. In the case of unleaded gasoline sold by Nam Kwong Oil, the updated price on February 28, a Saturday, showed that a liter was being sold for 14.20 patacas. This is the price without any discount and applied to all the brand's supply points in the territory. However, with discounts, the price could drop to 12.50 patacas per liter. The price update yesterday, March 4, maintained the original price at 14.20 patacas per liter. However, the gas station's special Wednesday promotion allowed you to pay just 11.82 patacas. The remaining prices for unleaded gasoline from the Shell, Esso and Caltex brands were updated the day before yesterday and yesterday. However, the previous updates had been made on December 15th, October 24th and July 6th, respectively, so it is not possible to make comparisons. In the case of Total, yesterday at noon, the price had not been updated since July 11th. ┬á Go up and down In relation to premium gasoline, prices registered opposite movements. According to the CC portal, only two brands are available on the market: Shell and Esso. In the case of the first, on February 26th, Thursday, the original price of fuel, that is, without discounts, at all eight stations was 16.61 patacas per liter. However, yesterday's update shows a reduction in the price of a liter of 8.2 percent, to 15.25 patacas per liter. In the case of Esso, on February 25th premium gasoline became more expensive. The price on February 25th was 16.50 patacas per liter and rose to 16.90 patacas per liter yesterday, an increase of 1.8 percent, or 40 cents. The portal also shows that it is not possible to make any comparison of diesel price movements. According to official information there are five brands on the market, Total, Shell, Esso, Caltex and Nam Kwong Oil. Shell and Catelx prices were updated on Tuesday to values ÔÇőÔÇőbetween 16.76 patacas per liter and 14.06/15.25 patacas per liter. However, the previous updates to these prices took place on July 15th and July 6th, respectively.

item.title: Income | JLL expects prices to stabilize
item.description: After prices fell ÔÇťsharplyÔÇŁ last year, real estate agency JLL points out that rents should stabilize throughout the year, both in terms of housing and commercial spaces. ┬á A market forecast released yesterday by real estate consultancy JLL indicates that rents for housing and commercial spaces in Macau are expected to stabilize this year, even with asset values ÔÇőÔÇőstill under pressure. In a report, the company indicates that residential prices, which fell sharply in 2025, are expected to remain stable in 2026, following government measures to ease mortgage burdens, including stamp duty exemptions and easing loan-to-property value ratios. Banks also cut interest rates late last year, helping to sustain demand. ÔÇťProperty developers reduced prices to boost sales last year and, with supportive policies now in place, the residential market should stabilize in the short term,ÔÇŁ said JLL Senior Director of Valuation and Risk in Macau, Mark Wong. ÔÇťBut limited long-term demand and the absence of major infrastructure projects will continue to weigh on the sector,ÔÇŁ he added, according to the report. The data shows that the equity value of housing fell 16.5 percent in 2025, while that of luxury properties fell 14.7 percent. Rents for conventional units fell 10.3 percent, although rents for high-end residences rose 1.1 percent. JLL predicts that both income and asset values ÔÇőÔÇőwill remain stable in 2026. The office market also faced difficulties last year, with general rents falling 3.4 percent and asset values ÔÇőÔÇőfalling 7.9 percent. The unemployment rate rose to 15.3 percent, reflecting weak demand. ┬á Falling asset value JLL ÔÇô the report also reads ÔÇô expects rents to stabilize in 2026, although asset value could fall by up to 5 percent. The retail sector also experienced pressure, with total sales falling 5.4 percent between January and September last year. Rents fell 0.9 percent in 2025. Although tourist areas are beginning to attract investors again, neighborhood stores remain under pressure due to high loan default rates. JLL predicts that retail asset values ÔÇőÔÇőcould fall another 5 percent this year. Macau's economy showed signs of resilience in 2025, with gross domestic product (GDP) growing 4.7 percent to 415.32 billion patacas and gaming revenue rising 9.1 percent to 247.4 billion patacas. Gaming revenue from the VIP segment increased by 24.1 percent to MOP67.98 billion. Visitor arrivals grew by 14.7 percent last year, to 40 million, driven mainly by tourists from the Interior. Despite the recovery in tourism and gaming, Wong warned of persistent structural challenges. ÔÇťEconomic growth has returned to pre-pandemic levels, but the drivers are concentrated in just a few industries,ÔÇŁ he said. ÔÇťSentiment in the commercial market is weak and the closure of satellite casinos will continue to pose challenges,ÔÇŁ he said.

item.title: Taiwan | Those responsible for the shopping center that exploded deny accusations
item.description: A trial is taking place in Taiwan that puts two people responsible for the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi shopping center in Taichung on the stand, where a gas explosion occurred in February last year, which resulted in five deaths and 38 injuries. The victims included three Macao SAR residents who lost their lives and four who were injured. The trial started yesterday, and the shopping center manager, the deputy manager of the parent company for the Taichung region (which has several shopping centers) and an employee pleaded not guilty, denying charges of negligent homicide, negligent wrongdoing and violating the occupational health and safety law. The shopping center in question is responsible for the largest revenue and flow of visitors for the entire group, which has centers in several locations in Taiwan. According to the Taiwanese newspaper Liberty Times, the prosecution argued that due to the size of the space, public safety should be implemented with the highest standards of rigor, but that this did not happen, with the company ignoring legal requirements and taking risks in the works for financial reasons that led to the explosion. The investigation revealed that those responsible did not request the necessary legal authorizations to carry out the work, a fire prevention plan was not drawn up, and the gas supply was not interrupted during the work. The trial continues this afternoon, with interviews with more defendants, 13 in total.

item.title: Light Rail | February breaks passenger record
item.description: After the successful registration in its first month of circulation with free travel for residents, the means of transport finally surpassed that passenger flow after five years Macau's light metro registered, on average, 33,100 passengers per day in February, the highest monthly number since its inauguration, the operator said yesterday. According to official data released by the Macau Light Metro Society, the average number of passengers increased by 10.3 percent compared to January and rose 30.8 percent compared to the same month in 2025. The light metro was inaugurated on December 10, 2019 and that month held the absolute record, with a daily average of 33 thousand passengers, and at that time travel was free. In February 2020, with the start of fare collection and the detection of the first cases of infection with the new coronavirus in Macau, the daily average number of passengers fell to 1,100. The light metro would record this minimum value again in July 2022, the month in which the city was in confinement for two weeks due to an outbreak of covid-19. In December 2024, the 2.2-kilometer extension of the light surface metro connecting Macau to neighboring Hengqin (Mountain Island) began operating. A month earlier, the line that goes to Seac Pai Van, a neighborhood in Coloane where the Macau Government built 60,000 public housing apartments, was inaugurated. Expansion little by little The light metro started with just one line, which ran only on the island of Taipa, with a length of 9.3 kilometers and 11 stations, with a frequency of ten to 15 minutes, for almost 17 hours a day. The metro connection to Barra, in the south of the Macau peninsula, via the lower level of the Sai Van bridge, began operating in December 2023. With the extension of the light metro, authorities predict that passenger volume will reach 137 thousand people per day in 2030. At the end of 2022, the Government launched tenders for the design and construction of the East Line of the light rail, which will connect the north of the Macau peninsula, where the main border with the Interior is located. At the end of January, the Secretary for Transport and Public Works, Raymond Tam Vai Man, said that the operator promised, later this year, to implement electronic payment, as well as a transfer system between buses and the light metro. A public consultation on the development of the light rail ended on February 28, which predicts that construction of the Eastern Line should be completed in 2029. The plans include a Southern Line, which will connect the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge border post to Barra station, a Western Line, from the Qingmao border to Barra, and a connection to the town of Coloane.

item.title: Real Estate | Association wants subsidy when buying first home
item.description: The Macau Property Development Commercial Association argues that the Government should subsidize the payment of interest rates on credits for purchasing a first home, covering 2 percent of this interest. The president of the association, Lok Wai Tak, understands that the measures approved by the Government, such as tax benefits and relaxation of maximum mortgage limits, were not enough to stop the fall in the real estate market and that more policies are needed for the sector to overcome the challenges it faces. In statements cited by the newspaper Ou Mun, the association leader also indicated that the prevailing sentiment in the market continues to be caution, while property sales and prices continue on a downward trend. As such, he called on the Government to take into account the real estate reality of Hong Kong, one of the most speculative markets in the world. The president of the International Real Estate Federation, Che Chan U, also pointed to the weakness of the global economy and the difficulties of small and medium-sized local companies as a scenario that does not help confidence in the real estate market. The leader is hopeful that the Sam Hou Fai Executive will introduce a residence-by-investment policy to stabilize property prices.

item.title: SJM | Maternity leave extension announced
item.description: With the latest announcement from the Stanley Ho-founded company, all game dealerships are following Sands China's example and increasing maternity leave from 70 to 90 days and paternity leave from five to seven days Since yesterday, all gaming concessionaires have adopted the policy of increasing maternity leave to 90 days and paternity leave to seven days. Confirmation of the new widespread practice in the gaming industry came after SJM Resorts announced the new measures for workers. ÔÇťAs of March 1, 2026, the company has officially increased parental benefits, increasing paid maternity leave from 70 to 90 days and paid paternity leave from five to seven days,ÔÇŁ it was announced. ÔÇťThis reinforcement actively supports the Macao SAR Government's policy of encouraging birth rates and further demonstrates the company's commitment to promoting a family-friendly workplaceÔÇŁ, it was added. The extensions of paid leave only apply to employees ÔÇťwith at least one year of serviceÔÇŁ, so if they do not reach this level they are subject to the previous regime. ÔÇťThe well-being and family happiness of our employees have always been at the core of our values, and a robust family support structure is fundamental to attracting and retaining the best talent,ÔÇŁ said SJM general manager Daisy Ho. ÔÇťThis latest improvement is a true reflection of our unwavering commitment to our people, and we hope that, by strengthening our parental benefits, SJM can be the most reliable pillar of support for all employees during the most significant moments of their livesÔÇŁ, he highlighted. General trend SJM was the last game concessionaire to announce the measure, in a trend that began with Sands China on February 25th. Similar announcements were made by Galaxy, Wynn Macau, MGM China, and Melco. However, the measures do not happen in a vacuum. The Government is carrying out a public consultation to increase maternity leave from 70 to 90 days and also the annual vacation days for workers from the current six to a maximum of 12. The vacation days now depend on the number of years the employee has been with the company and for every two years they accumulate one more day. Concessionaires are thus anticipating measures that could come into force by next year, but which still need to be approved by the Legislative Assembly, which should happen throughout this year.

item.title: ZELENSKY VS ZELENSKY
item.description: 10/27/22 A Ukrainian pensioner, with the same name as the president, blames Zelensky for the prolongation of the conflict that destroyed his village. Zelensky, the villager, who never left his land, is now mortified because his favorite gravedigger died in the bomber. This Zelensky, a villager, represents half the world that recriminates the other Zelensky, the villain, for not wanting to negotiate with Putin. Negotiate what? The invader enters our house, covers his wife and daughter (cf. recent UN report on the rapes in Ukraine, in this ÔÇťspecial operationÔÇŁ), an act that forced us to watch, and then we say to him: hi there, we are for peace, but we have to negotiate: you still haven't said what name you want to give the children! A peace that is not dignified is nothing more than a cessation of rights. As serious as the Russophobia that Putin inflicts on his people. ┬á 10/28/22 At a lunch with several guests, I heard a friend, who declared herself a spiritualist, explaining the particularities of her belief, in a calm and balanced way, I would even say, without a hint of ÔÇťirrationalismÔÇŁ. He spoke long and serenely, devoid of any fanatical proselytism. ÔÇťHere is someone whose belief has brought thoughtfulness!ÔÇŁ I thought, a rare alliance. However, at a certain moment a sentence comes out that horrifies me. It says, ÔÇťthere is no chance, nothing happens by chance!ÔÇŁ, and I glimpse the voracious maws of holism. Because holism has a pathological aspect, like all good things, a shadow side. Two examples: in Hinduism there is less sensitivity to existential dramas, if that child suffers in this life this is just an expression of rigorous karmic symmetry, an effect of the acts he committed in his previous life. In Africa, it is not accepted that death could have been accidental, and many women are accused by the family of the deceased of having caused the death of their husband, in an act of manifest dementia, and, in the blatant majority of cases, a huge injustice for the wifeÔÇŐÔÇöÔÇŐmadness that spreads and infiltrates the social fabric. Buddhism introduced Compassion and Christianity introduced Caritas, trying to break with this logic, but in many other beliefs and religions the cage of causalities prevails over sensitivity to experience, the need to respond to the now. It is a tremendous logic that transforms the world into a Platonic stage, where we are nothing more than the shadows of somethingÔÇŐÔÇöÔÇŐan inextricable law -, in a determinism that calcifies all singularities, but that strangely fascinates many. The conquest of modernity was anchored in the conquest of chance and randomness, removing the incidences of a life from this fierce determinism that enclosed the world in a web. When the Renaissance freed the body from the influence of the stars, at that moment sexuality was relieved of guilt and individualism and its volition were able to emerge. There is an unequivocal moral implication in the phrase ÔÇťthere is no such thing as chance, nothing happens by chance!ÔÇŁ, which should function as a key to self-knowledge and self-judgment, however, this phrase is usually used to seek a reason for things outside of us, in others. It's an alibi. Today there is a┬áholistic temptation in the world, just as there was a satanic temptation in the Middle Ages, without much reflection on its consequences. Because it's not enough to want to connect everything in a resonant chain of affection. Even for the most unexpected: affection itself can kill. I realize, thanks to that cultured, balanced, intelligent woman, that ÔÇťthe fightÔÇŁ took place at the beginning of this century. One of the great mistakes of the 20th century was the false dichotomy between ÔÇťrationalism and irrationalismÔÇŁ, a debate that extended to all fields, including art. First, rationality (the positive side of reason) was confused with rationalism (the pathological aspect of reason), and articulated in this lack of discernment, irrationalism was confused with freedom. Almost all 20th century art, with Surrealism at its head, made this mistake. The fallacy still exists despite the emergence of ÔÇťirrationalismÔÇŁ and the consequent relativist currents that followed it having been consolidated everywhere. Essentially, much of what happened in the 20th century revolved around this clash between two paradigms: Rationalism versus Irationalism. Now, I see, we must save indeterminacy, chance, randomness from the terrible temptation of the cage of causalities. Holism, in its pathological form, may be reductionism that sets itself up as a new ÔÇťeschatological narrativeÔÇŁ. Is it ÔÇťby chanceÔÇŁ that holism emerges at a time when neo-liberalism and its cynical contempt for the human person is besieging everything, all freedoms, all acquired rights? Holistic awareness has brought a positive impact to greater ecological awareness, but followed with scholastic rigor, it stifles reality under the blanket of a determinism that is much more than uncomfortable: all great authoritarian ideologies are holistic. My friend will know how to combine her holistic and transpersonal belief with the freedom and responsibility that each moment asks of usÔÇŐÔÇöÔÇŐbut how many will make the distinction, under a Law that explains and encompasses everything? How many will abandon themselves to┬áwhat is? The awareness of the indeterminate brings tragic awareness, as the Greeks knew, but it also brings agency and the courage of decision. And we cannot give up on this. Furthermore, as Drummond shows in the poem ÔÇťQuadrilhaÔÇŁ, by Carlos Drummond de Andrade: ┬źJo├úo loved Teresa who loved Raimundo/ who loved Maria who loved Joaquim who loved Lili/ who didn't love anyone./ Jo├úo went to the United States, Teresa to the convent,/ Raimundo died of a disaster, Maria stayed with her aunt,/ Joaquim committed suicide and Lili married J. Pinto Fernandes/ who had not entered the story.┬╗ Real always runs outside the track, completely outside our concepts. That's what matters to usÔÇŐÔÇöÔÇŐa J. Pinto Fernandes emerges at the exact moment when we thought we had everything under control.

item.title: From Nobel and others
item.description: 9/10/22 When reading many people's reactions when this year's Nobel Prize for Literature was announced, I couldn't help but remember this comment from Jean-Pierre Sim├ęon, in relation to another Nobel Prize: ┬źAn editor of a large national daily newspaper wrote five lines of indignation at the fact that the Nobel Prize for Literature had been awarded to Thomas Trastr├Âmer, a complete stranger. But unknown to whom? How did it not occur to this literary columnist, patented and paid to do his job, that his apparent ignorance was a matter of professional misconduct? Transtr├Âmer had been translated into many languages, it was available in French.┬╗ There is always the chorus of those who wait for the Prize to be announced to complain about not having been invited to be on the jury, there is a subgroup of angry people who profess ÔÇťI have never read it and I wonÔÇÖt even bother to read itÔÇŁ; It is a less than humble fauna, the one that inhabits social networks. I don't always know all the winners ÔÇô I didn't know about the existence of Louise Gl├╝ck, who I think is a great poet, or Olga Tokarczuk, for example ÔÇô but this only brings me the comfort of knowing that the world is not a soap opera where everyone knows everyone else and the joy of recognizing that the yardstick of the world is not measured with my short measure and that, on the contrary, it gives me a new opportunity to face the unknown and learn from it. And perhaps the most stupid reaction was that of the intelligent man who wrote, But are there still people who read French?; simply confirming that today there is a new type of ignorance that is being raised with abandon: that of those who only read English. I would also prefer that, in French, Pascal Quignard, Linda L├¬ or even Sollers were awarded, or, among the new ones, Mathias ├ënard, but that does not detract from the dignity of Annie Ernaux's work (and it would have been enough to have written ÔÇťLes Ann├ęesÔÇŁ). There are books of hers that I like less, like the vulgar ÔÇťUma Paix├úo SimplesÔÇŁ, but that happens with almost all authors, they have pivotal books and others that are like breaks in their itinerary. I recommend reading a beautiful book of interviews with her, ÔÇťLÔÇÖ├ęcriture comme un couteauÔÇŁ. 11/10/22 Yesterday an article appeared in the morning newspaper ÔÇťNot├şciasÔÇŁ, in Maputo, reporting that the Pen Club Narrative Prize had been awarded to my wife, Teresa Noronha, for her soap opera ÔÇťTornadoÔÇŁ, which had already won the Maria Velho da Costa Prize. The summary they made of the book does not even deserve comments, as it is pathetic, but what is important to highlight is the note of disqualification regarding its ÔÇťMozambicannessÔÇŁ when they call it Luso-Mozambican. It's not that it's inaccurate, but, for those who know the context, it's a touch naughty. Because finally, following the same logic, Adelino Tim├│teo should be called Tsonga-Mozambican, ├ülvaro Taruma Ronga-Mozambican, and Macua-Mozambican for writers from the north, etc., etc. Which doesn't appear to happen. At one point, in the novel, she writes: ┬źOur color was never the dominant one. In colonial times we were not white, we were dressed in monh├ęs, mugs of washed asses, the pejorative term to speak of a son of Goan and Portuguese. In the post-colonial period, I was not black and if, in Lisbon, they took me for Brazilian or Cape Verdean, in France they were lost in thought about where I was from and were surprised when they discovered that I was African. There I had to explain the wanderings of my DNA before settling in Mozambique. It took me a long time to accept that it was like that because yes, and that it was no curse that that was the case; on the contrary, I could even discover intense patterns of fantasy and freedom in this harlequin cloak that is our genealogyÔÇŽÔÇŁ In modern-day Mozambique, it means being whiteÔÇŽ and that immediately takes precedence over what you do or what you are. It's sad, but as Naipaul warned, in these parts race is everything and people live as deeply immersed in it as, in other places, in religion. 12/10/22 Bruno Latour, an anthropologist who studied the sciences and their procedures and legitimations, law, technique and religions and who entertained himself by turning his back on the dogmas of the ÔÇťmodernsÔÇŁ, has died. For him, the place, the role of the specialist, was defined more by the perplexity he embodied than by the supposed truth that would sustain him. He proposed, as an alternative to the goodness of multiculturalism and its equivocal clich├ę ÔÇťall different, all equalÔÇŁ, a return to ÔÇťdiplomacyÔÇŁ, given that this, precisely, does not start from the assumption that ÔÇťwe are unified because we share the same natureÔÇŁ but simply highlights that we ÔÇťstill do not share a common worldÔÇŁ; It is necessary that we deal head-on with the ÔÇťabyss of disagreementÔÇŁ that separates us, instead of trying to deceive it. And about politics he said, boldly: ┬źIf transparency is demanded from politicians, we kill them; if anyone demands fidelity to what the other was or what he says, kill him; If a mimetic relationship is required between what the crowd wants and what its representative says, representation is killed. These must therefore, by definition, betray, deceive, modify, distort the word. Without this ability to bend the word and betray, political language would not exist. I put forward the perfectly plausible hypothesis that political vocabulary will become as inaccessible as the religious language of the last twenty, three or fifty years.┬╗ (in ÔÇťUn Monde Pluriel mais CommunÔÇŁ). I believe we will be needed to decipher this frantically complex world that has fallen to us.

item.title: Fuxi
item.description: O my friends, companions of these disastrous journeys through countries as old as the stars and the Moon, and as strange as the deepest interior of our wretched souls, what surprises still await us, what secret magics will we still have to overcome, what horrors will arise before our pupils dilated with astonishment and terror? Increasingly closer to our precarious existences, howls the monster of war, which threatens to merge humanity once and for all with the earth, incessantly traversed by the squalid specter of hunger and the invisible evil of ten thousand pestilences. Oh damned human, who believes you are superior to Nature itself, but then you reveal yourself, time and time again, incapable of simply ordering your existence and do not understand that you are possessed by fickle anger and are a slave to your own ambition! You are not satisfied with white jade or resplendent gold, there is no treasure that quenches your inextinguishable thirst for command over other men, animals and things; for eternal anguish dwells in you; in you the expelled serpents crawl; for you grows, powerful and impressive, the most fertile ivy of Evil! This is perhaps why on the Mount they call the White Deer, where one of the eight immortals on a sacred mount soared through the heavens towards Penglai, lives the fuxi, a bird whose shape resembles that of a rooster, although topped by a human head. What strange copulations, what monstrous embraces, what terrible procedures must have occurred to make the existence of this sinister animal possible? We don't know, but all it takes is for our exhausted mind to dare to come up with an answer, and then we feel our skin covered in horrendous goosebumps and our heart races in parade, like a wild horse whipped by the night whip of fear, unresponsive to reins and containment. Let men refrain from visiting Monte do Veado, because although there are riches capable of calming the wildest ambitions, if they have the unfortunate luck of catching a glimpse of a fuxi, it is certain that a war will soon break out and the cultivated fields will perish, because of it the tame animals will be beheaded, because of it the best young men of two generations will be sacrificed and the pride of the countries will be destroyed. The fuxi makes a sound reminiscent of its name. If you hear it, dark night or clear day, be on your way, because the path you are on is the surest path to misfortune!

item.title: Procrastinators Club
item.description: While rereading Easter, because of an article I'm preparing, I come across this excerpt, taken from ÔÇťThe Universal ManÔÇŁ: ┬źEach man, morally, is one and absolute; but he has to live with attenuating his personality. (ÔÇŽ) Coexistence is only possible between people reduced to a negative or hollow presence, which constantly gives way. Hence, the emptiness of society, an immense ghost made up of countless dead people. A superior man is antisocial or criminal. His destiny is prison and the desertÔÇŁ. I hesitate to think whether Pascoaes reflects here on the political-social conditions that muzzle, and, worse, in a sad country like Portugal in the middle of the Estado Novo (and the book is from 1931) where any possibilities of spontaneity and frankness in human relationships were restricted, or if it merely lends to the behavior of (oppressed) men a metaphysical framework, an ÔÇťessenceÔÇŁ, declared in the presumption of each being, in isolation, ÔÇťone and absoluteÔÇŁ. Sometimes, in addition to an excess of Spirituality (or Saudade), Pascoaes lacks the world. He is a great poet, but he is a poet who takes Ampoules-of-Great-Ideas and who therefore writes often trapped by a programmatic furor, ceasing to have pores; It is not uncommon for a glass to be erected between him and reality, the same glass that made him write in O Bailado, from 1921: ┬źEverything is a ghost. There are only clouds, clouds of voices, clouds of souls, of afflictions and tragedies! Clouds and more clouds, appearances and more appearances! And a divine lightning that pierces them, the instantaneous Apparition that appears and throws us to the ground, dazzled!┬╗ Sometimes I felt like reminding him, as Ant├│nio S├ęrgio did, in the article ÔÇťRegeneration and Tradition, Moral and EconomyÔÇŁ, which he dedicated to Pascoaes: ┬źPascoais, Pascoais my dear friend: you are a pure, excellent and noble poet, but also a victim of this social environment, like us all: of this horrible isolation that you praise and I curse┬╗. ┬źAs a child, he stole two blackbirds┬╗, says Jacinto do Prado Coelho, he stole them from a nest, and he felt remorse for that all his life. The Person, few more mischiefs are known to him. Only in ├ülvaro de Campos does a hint of wickedness and obscenity emerge: ┬źAh, and the ordinary and dirty people, who always look the same,/ Who use swear words as usual words,/ Whose sons steal from grocery store doors/ And whose daughters at the age of eight ÔÇô and I think this is beautiful and I love it! ÔÇô/ Decent-looking men masturbate in stairwells.ÔÇŁ (Triumphal ode). Both poets, driven by a certain idea of ÔÇőÔÇősanctity, had the same inability to break the siege of their skin with the overflow of someone else's dam, the same curse of sublimating the concrete ferocity of eros with the gentleness of the verses. Obviously, they are both ÔÇťbigger than lifeÔÇŁ, but sometimes it makes me unnerved to watch, from my position as a hatter at the Procrastinators Club, the tantrums between them, just to see who occupies, in the internal organization, the place of President and Treasurer. Although Pessoa guessed more things, for example, what he wrote in the lesser-known Ode Martial and which is reflected in the situation we live in today, deferred, with his heart in his hands: ┬źMARTIAL ODE (ÔÇŽ) Helahoho! helahoho! The poor widow's sewing machine who was bayoneted... She sewed indefinitely in the afternoonÔÇŽ The table where the old people played, Everything mixed, everything mixed with bodies, with blood, All one river, one wave, one dragging horror. Helahoho! helahoho! I unearthed the child's tin train pressed into the middle of the [road, And I cried like all the mothers in the world about the horror of life. My pantheistic feet tripped over the sewing machine [widow who was killed with a bayonet And that poor instrument of peace stuck a spear in my heart. Yes, I was the one to blame for everything, I was the soldier for them all Who killed, raped, burned and broke, It was me and my shame and my remorse like a shadow [deformed They travel around the world like Ashavero, But behind my steps there are footsteps the size of infinity And a physical fear of being accountable to God makes me close my eyes. [eyes suddenly. (ÔÇŽ) I ordered, captain, to shoot the trembling peasants, I let the daughters of all fathers tied to trees be raped, Now I saw that it was inside my heart that all this happened, And everything scalds and suffocates and I can't move without it [everything be the same. God have mercy on me, I didn't have it from anyone! L├Ç-BAS, JE NE SAIS O├ÖÔÇŽÔÇŁ What is clear today, at this disastrous moment in which Cristiano & Companhia failed in their mission in Braga, and in which the self-sabotage of Russia's gas pipelines reminds us that Putin will be willing to do anything to maintain dominance over the areas endorsed, is that, bomb for bomb, the ÔÇťbombÔÇŁ of the Fifth Empire was preferable. However, even in this, my dears, we were irresolute, a little more on the side of piela than determined, and we procrastinated.

item.title: CPPCC | Open main annual political meeting
item.description: China yesterday kicked off its main annual political meeting with the opening of the session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), the consultative body that precedes the inauguration, on Thursday, of the National People's Assembly, a legislative body. Chinese President Xi Jinping participated in the inaugural session held at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, accompanied by other members of the leadership of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). During the meeting, the work on the agenda was approved and Wang Huning, considered one of the regime's main ideologues and close to Xi, presented the activity report of the consultative body's standing committee. The opening of the so-called ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ, China's biggest annual political event, takes place at the beginning of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), which will define the country's economic and strategic priorities for the next five years. Tomorrow, Prime Minister Li Qiang is expected to announce the economic growth target for 2026, which, according to analysts, could be between 4.5 percent and 5 percent. The annual meeting also takes place after the expulsion, in recent weeks, of several senior military officials from both the National Popular Assembly and the consultative body itself, as part of the anti-corruption campaign that has reduced the leadership of the armed forces. The Defense budget, which in last year's session increased by 7.2 percent year-on-year, and political messages aimed at the Armed Forces should also focus part of the attention during the debates. ┬á Global tensions The session is also taking place in a context of growing international tensions, following attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, and before a planned visit by US President Donald Trump to China, which has not yet been officially confirmed by Beijing. In the first contacts with the local press, some members of the consultative body highlighted topics such as industrial and technological modernization. The president of car manufacturer Geely, Li Shufu, called, in statements cited by The Paper newspaper, to avoid ÔÇťsenseless competitionÔÇŁ in the electric vehicle sector, while other delegates highlighted advances in quantum technology and the need to accelerate the regulation of autonomous vehicles and humanoid robotics. During the session of the legislative body, Foreign Minister Wang Yi is expected to hold his usual annual press conference, an event often used to anticipate the tone of Chinese diplomacy in an increasingly volatile international context.

item.title: Teaching | China prepares law that imposes Mandarin as the main language
item.description: China is preparing to pass a law that will make Mandarin the main language of instruction for ethnic minorities. According to the British newspaper Financial Times, the draft Law for the Promotion of Ethnic Unity and Progress is expected to be approved this week during the annual meeting of the Chinese parliament, the National People's Congress. The diploma establishes that Mandarin will become the main language of instruction in schools and universities attended by minorities, allowing languages ÔÇőÔÇősuch as Tibetan, Uighur or Mongolian to be taught only as a second language, no longer being able to be used in core subjects. The text also provides for legal action against individuals or organizations, inside or outside China, that ÔÇťundermine national unityÔÇŁ or incite ÔÇťseparatismÔÇŁ, and states that ethnic unity ÔÇťshould not be interfered with by external forces under the pretext of ethnicity, religion or human rightsÔÇŁ. Analistas citados pelo jornal consideram que a nova lei representa uma mudan├ža estrutural na abordagem do Estado ├ás minorias. For critics, the legislation is part of a broader effort at cultural assimilation that has been intensifying under the leadership of Xi Jinping, in a country where the Han majority represents around 90 percent of the population of 1.4 billion inhabitants, but which officially recognizes 56 ethnic groups and several languages ÔÇőÔÇőwith their own writing systems. Neil Thomas, a researcher at the Asia Society's China Analysis Center, said the law "will expand the legal basis for restricting religious, cultural and political activities among minority groups." Wang Yanzhong, a researcher linked to the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, argued that the diploma aims to provide legal safeguards for the Communist Party's work on ethnic matters and contribute to ÔÇťmaintaining the security and stability of border regions and ethnic areasÔÇŁ. The academic also argued that Mandarin offers greater educational and professional advantages, arguing that the number of speakers of minority languages ÔÇőÔÇőis decreasing among younger generations.

item.title: China / USA | Reinforcement of investment before TrumpÔÇÖs arrival
item.description: China and the United States are discussing ways to reactivate reciprocal investment as part of preparations for the US President's planned visit to Beijing, the Hong Kong newspaper South China Morning Post reported yesterday. Citing anonymous sources, the newspaper said that the signing of agreements in the area of ÔÇőÔÇőinvestment could be one of the few tangible results of the trip, announced by Washington for March 31 to April 2, but not yet officially confirmed by Beijing. Uncertainty surrounding the visit and possible results has increased in recent days, following attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran, a country of which China is the main trading partner and largest buyer of oil, as well as one of its most important diplomatic supports. According to the newspaper, although there are no specific agreements yet, both Beijing and Washington have expressed interest in structures such as joint companies with clear governance models, licensing agreements or formats with less exposure in terms of intellectual property, capable of resisting political and regulatory scrutiny. One of the sources cited points to the agreement reached in 2023 between Ford and the Chinese CATL, the world's largest manufacturer of batteries for electric vehicles, as a ÔÇťpossible modelÔÇŁ, which allowed the North American company to license lithium iron phosphate battery technology for use in the United States. Among the points of friction, North American representatives insisted on the need to improve access to the Chinese market, where foreign companies have been denouncing unequal treatment for years. Beijing called for greater protection for its investments in the face of losses and withdrawals driven by greater scrutiny and tariffs, also addressing initial public offerings by Chinese companies in North American markets. The United States has traditionally been one of the main destinations for Chinese foreign investment, which reached a maximum of US$17 billion in 2016, falling to around US$6.6 billion in 2024. In total, China invested more than US$90 billion in the world's largest economy. According to data released by Beijing, US investment in China fell 18.5 percent in 2024, to around US$2.7 billion. According to one of the sources, it was conveyed that Chinese investment is welcome, as long as it is in sectors considered non-sensitive. However, for this to happen, it would be necessary to ÔÇťchange the narrativeÔÇŁ and mitigate the discourse of the ÔÇťChinese threatÔÇŁ dominant in the United States. New round Last week, the Chinese Ministry of Commerce announced the holding of a sixth round of trade negotiations with Washington. Although the date and location have not been confirmed, the talks are expected to serve as a precursor to Trump's visit. The South China Morning Post also said that the trip could allow the extension of the one-year trade truce agreed in October between Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, which helped to temporarily ease tensions generated by the tariff escalation that began after the Republican's return to the White House.

item.title: Tehran | China accuses the United States and Israel of ÔÇťinciting regime changeÔÇŁ
item.description: China yesterday accused the United States and Israel of ÔÇťinciting regime changeÔÇŁ in Iran, following the attacks launched on February 28, classifying the assassination of the leader of the Islamic republic, Ali Khamenei, as ÔÇťunacceptableÔÇŁ. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning reiterated at a press conference that the US and Israeli offensive ÔÇťviolates international law and the basic norms that govern international relationsÔÇŁ. The statements come after telephone conversations held the day before by the head of Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi, with his counterparts from Iran, Oman and France. Mao structured the Chinese position on three axes: immediate cessation of military operations, return to dialogue and opposition to unilateral actions. The official stressed that Beijing supports Iran in defending its sovereignty, while taking into account the ÔÇťlegitimate security concernsÔÇŁ of the Gulf countries. Regarding the nuclear issue, the spokesperson stated that China respects ÔÇťIran's legitimate right to the peaceful use of nuclear energyÔÇŁ and argued that the issue must return ÔÇťto the path of political and diplomatic resolutionÔÇŁ. China, Iran's main trading partner and largest importer of its oil, had already condemned on Sunday the death of Ali Khamenei during the offensive led by Israel and the United States. Since Saturday, Israel, in coordination with Washington, has been bombing several positions in Iran, claiming to seek to destroy arsenals and ballistic missile production capabilities and put an end to the ayatollah regime. Iran | Beijing calls for the preservation of security in the Strait of Hormuz China yesterday appealed to all parties involved in the war in the Middle East to guarantee the safety of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, warning of the risk of impact on the world economy. ÔÇťChina urges all parties to immediately cease military operations, avoid further escalation of tensions, maintain the security of shipping routes in the Strait of Hormuz and prevent the situation from having a broader impact on the global economy,ÔÇŁ said Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning, at a regular press conference. The Strait of Hormuz, which connects the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman, is one of the world's main energy arteries, through which around a fifth of the oil traded globally by sea passes through. At the beginning of 2025, China was the main destination for oil that crosses that strategic route. In recent years, the country was also the largest importer of Iranian crude, in a context of closer economic relations between Beijing and Tehran. The statements come at a time of strong regional instability, following the attacks launched on February 28 by the United States and Israel against Iran, which responded with missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles ('drones') against North American bases and targets in Gulf countries. The increased risk in the region led several shipping companies to suspend or divert routes in the Strait of Hormuz, fueling fears of disruptions in energy supplies and a further rise in crude oil prices. Beijing has reiterated the need for an immediate ceasefire and return to dialogue, while underlining the importance of preserving the stability of global supply chains.

item.title: Shanghai Cooperation Organization considers use of force against Tehran ÔÇťunacceptableÔÇŁ
item.description: The Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) yesterday classified the use of force as ÔÇťunacceptableÔÇŁ and called for the resolution of the conflict with Iran through dialogue and respect for the legitimate interests of all parties. In a statement published on its official portal, the SCO highlighted ÔÇťthe need to guarantee the national sovereignty, security and territorial integrity of IranÔÇŁ and expressed support for all parties to ÔÇťact with moderation and immediately put an end to actions that could worsen the situationÔÇŁ. ÔÇťThe SCO Member States strongly urge the United Nations and its Security Council to adopt immediate measures to confront actions that undermine international peace and securityÔÇŁ, states the text. The organization's countries also expressed ÔÇťtheir most sincere condolences to the families of the victims of the attacksÔÇŁ and extended ÔÇťsolidarity and support to the Iranian Government and peopleÔÇŁ. Over the weekend, the group had already offered condolences to the Iranian authorities over the death of the supreme leader of the Islamic republic, Ali Khamenei, in a US and Israeli attack. Half the world The Shanghai Cooperation Organization, which does not have mutual defense clauses like NATO, includes China, Russia, India, Pakistan, Iran, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan, bringing together around 40 percent of the world's population, as well as observer countries and dialogue partners, such as Egypt, Turkey, Myanmar and Azerbaijan. According to the Iranian Red Crescent, more than 555 people died in the Islamic republic following attacks launched since Saturday by Israeli and American forces, including 180 in a bombing attack on a school in the southern city of Minab.

item.title: Gulf | Chinese MNE expects countries to ÔÇťstrengthen their independenceÔÇŁ
item.description: Wang Yi condemned the attacks in the Middle East in a conversation with his Omani counterpart in which he called on countries in the region to reject interference from abroad. The Chinese official reiterated Beijing's readiness to play a constructive role in restoring peace The head of Chinese diplomacy yesterday called on the Gulf countries to strengthen their independence and reject external interference, in a conversation with his Omani counterpart following Iranian attacks against several states in the region. According to a statement released by Chinese diplomacy, Wang Yi stated that ÔÇťChina appreciates Oman's active mediation to promote negotiations between Iran and the United States, as well as its great efforts to safeguard regional peaceÔÇŁ. The head of Chinese diplomacy maintained that ÔÇťthe United States and Israel deliberately provoked a war against Iran, which clearly violates the purposes and principles of the United Nations CharterÔÇŁ. Wang declared that ÔÇťthe urgent task now is to immediately end military actions to prevent further spread of the conflict and prevent an irreversible escalationÔÇŁ, adding that China ÔÇťis also willing to play a constructive roleÔÇŁ. "China hopes that Gulf countries will develop good neighborly relations and strengthen solidarity and coordination, so that they can fully control their own future," he said. Sayyid Badr Albusaidi declared that ÔÇťnegotiations between Iran and the United States had achieved unprecedented progressÔÇŁ, but that, ÔÇťunfortunatelyÔÇŁ, Washington and Tel Aviv ÔÇťignored the results of the talks and started a warÔÇŁ, according to the statement. In recent weeks, the Sultanate of Oman had mediated contacts between the United States and Iran over the Iranian nuclear program. Iranian response Iran launched attacks with missiles and unmanned aerial vehicles ('drones') against North American bases and targets in Gulf countries such as Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Bahrain, in response to the offensive launched by the United States and Israel against its territory. Tehran maintains that these actions are part of its retaliation against what it considers direct aggression and against the US military presence in the region. Wang also spoke yesterday by phone with his Iranian counterpart, Abbas Araqchi, to whom he assured Beijing's support in defending Iran's sovereignty, security and territorial integrity, in the first firm show of support since the attacks by the United States and Israel. China, Iran's main trading partner and largest importer of its oil, condemned on Sunday the death of Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei during the offensive led by Israel and the United States. Since Saturday, Israel has been bombing, in coordination with Washington, several positions in Iran, claiming to seek to destroy arsenals and ballistic missile production capabilities and put an end to the ayatollah regime.

item.title: Cooperation | India and Canada with energy and uranium supply agreement
item.description: India and Canada announced yesterday, in New Delhi, a cooperation agreement on minerals and the supply of uranium, essential for nuclear energy. The uranium supply agreement, as part of the strategic partnership between the two countries, has a value of 2.6 billion Canadian dollars. India, a major energy consumer and the world's most populous country with 1.4 billion people, aims to increase nuclear capacity from eight gigawatts to 100 gigawatts by 2047. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi considered that a historic agreement had been reached in the area of ÔÇőÔÇőcivil nuclear energy, establishing a long-term supply of uranium. Modi added that the two countries will also work together on small modular reactors and advanced reactors. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said Canada is ÔÇťwell positionedÔÇŁ to be a ÔÇťreliable supplierÔÇŁ of liquefied natural gas (LNG) from the country's west coast. The agreements, which also covered the technology sector and the promotion of renewable energy, were announced after a meeting in New Delhi between Modi and Carney. Carney's visit represents an improvement in relations between Canada and India, which had deteriorated in 2023. At the time, Ottawa accused New Delhi of conducting a campaign of intimidation against 'Sikh' activists residing in Canada, allegations rejected by India. At the meeting, Carney stated that the Canadian and Indian governments collaborated more in the last year than in the last two decades, stressing that ÔÇťit is not simply about renewing a relationshipÔÇŁ. Last year, the two countries had agreed to resume negotiations on a comprehensive economic partnership agreement. ÔÇťOur objective is to reach 50 billion dollars in bilateral tradeÔÇŁ, said Modi.

item.title: Persian Gulf | Cosco reorganizes routes and searches for safe waters
item.description: Chinese state shipping company Cosco Shipping announced that it is reorganizing the route of its ships in the Persian Gulf in light of the insecurity situation in the Middle East and restrictions on transit in the Strait of Hormuz. In a notice to customers dated March 1, the company indicated that vessels that have already entered the Gulf, after completing their operations ÔÇťand when it is safe to do soÔÇŁ, were instructed to head to safe waters and to remain anchored or drifting. Ships bound for the region received guidance to prioritize navigation safety, including reducing speed, remaining in protected anchorages or complying with new operational instructions, according to the statement. The company adds that it is evaluating contingency plans for cargo on board the affected ships, including possible unloading alternatives in other ports. The announcement comes after Iran warned that transit through the Strait of Hormuz is no longer safe, following the conflict triggered after the attacks launched on February 28 by the United States and Israel against the Islamic republic. The Iranian warning and the increased risk led, in practice, to the suspension or diversion of routes by some large shipping companies, such as Maersk and the Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC). Yesterday, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning stated that Hormuz is an ÔÇťimportant international channel for the trade of goods and energyÔÇŁ, stressing that preserving its security corresponds to the ÔÇťcommon interests of the international communityÔÇŁ.

item.title: ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ | Beijing prepares to release new five-year plan
item.description: The so-called ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ start this week in Beijing. At stake, in a meeting that brings together around 3,000 delegates from across the country in the Chinese capital, will be the guidelines for economic, social, diplomatic and military policies for the next five years This week, China begins the so-called ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ, the country's main annual political event, with emphasis on the presentation of the 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030). Thousands of delegates from across the country gather at the Grand Palace of the People, in Beijing, next to Tiananmen Square, to approve legislation and formalize decisions previously defined by the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), which governs the country. The meeting will make it possible to publicize the new five-year plan, a document guiding economic, social, diplomatic, political and military policies for the next five years. The plan must present structural responses to various challenges, from weak domestic demand and the crisis in the real estate sector to restrictions on access to advanced technologies imposed by the United States and trade disputes with Washington and the European Union. The plenary session of the National People's Congress (NPC), China's highest legislative body, begins on Thursday and lasts for about a week. At the same time, the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), a consultative body that includes representatives from various sectors of society, is meeting from Wednesday. Prime Minister Li Qiang is expected to announce on Thursday the official economic growth target for 2026. In 2025, the Chinese economy grew 5 percent, in line with the government's objective, but at the lowest rates in recent decades. Analysts anticipate that the target for this year could be between 4.5 percent and 5 percent. In recent years, Beijing has advocated a reorientation of the economic model towards greater dependence on domestic consumption, reducing the traditional focus on exports and public investment. However, uncertainty in the property market and youth unemployment continue to encourage household savings. Experts believe that the new plan should reinforce the focus on high technologies, the ecological transition and the security of supply chains. Steve Tsang, director of the SOAS China institute at the University of London, stated that the main line should deepen the orientation already defined by Chinese President Xi Jinping, without significant structural changes to the political or economic model. Sarah Tan, economist at MoodyÔÇÖs Analytics, considered that the strategy signals a transition from a model based on debt to one focused on innovation, but warns that a sustainable recovery will require greater social protection, increased income and a resolution of the housing crisis. Other challenges China also faces a demographic challenge, with the population declining for the third year in a row. The Government has announced birth support measures, including subsidies and expansion of childcare services, but the impact has been limited. The Defense budget should also be revealed during the sessions, at a time when the Government is conducting a broad anti-corruption campaign within the Armed Forces. The ÔÇťTwo SessionsÔÇŁ are seen as a key moment to signal Beijing's strategic priorities to the country and the international community.

item.title: Miguel Carvalho, author of the book ÔÇťPor Dentro do ChegaÔÇŁ: ÔÇťChega attracted everything and a pair of bootsÔÇŁ
item.description:
Journalist Miguel Carvalho has long been investigating Chega, the far-right political party founded by Andr├ę Ventura. Guest of the ÔÇťRota das LetrasÔÇŁ Literary Festival, Miguel Carvalho comes to Macau to talk about the book ÔÇťPor Dentro do Chega ÔÇô A Face Oculta da Extrema-Rireita em PortugalÔÇŁ and the growth of populism He will share the session ÔÇťFrom Mistake to PopulismÔÇŁ with another journalist, Jo├úo Miguel Tavares, who wrote a work about Jos├ę S├│crates. What parallels do you find between Socrates and Chega? I find it quite curious that the debate between these two books [ÔÇťJos├ę S├│crates ÔÇô Ascens├úo (1957-2005)ÔÇŁ and ÔÇťPor Dentro do ChegaÔÇŁ] took place. This is because Andr├ę Ventura himself assumed, although he later denied it, but it is recorded, that he voted for Jos├ę S├│crates in the first term. I describe in the book the exemplary way in which Ventura, in some way, looks at himself in relation to his own political persona, in the positive characteristics he saw in Jos├ę S├│crates, in relation to the idea of ÔÇőÔÇőthe politician who came to change everything and anything. But Jos├ę S├│crates is a hated figure within Chega, he is, perhaps, the politician that Chega refers to most to identify the bad guys in politics. In many cases, S├│crates was the motivation why many joined Chega. In my research I found many activists who voted for Jos├ę S├│crates, believing it to be the best thing that could happen to Portuguese politics. The book is in its ninth edition in Portugal. Did you expect this reaction from the public, taking into account the growth of Chega's electorate? There is a dimension to this that, for me, is a little unreal. We are talking about a country like Portugal, which has marginal reading rates, and we already have 36 thousand copies in circulation in total. But this reaction from Chega's electoral universe, both from people who have already left or who are still there, is not a complete surprise to me. I received a lot of messages via email or Facebook from people who had either left Chega or were still there, but who respected my journalistic work, regardless of what they might think of me as a citizen or my convictions. People who were reading the book with a critical mind. I know people who read the book and left Chega, and others who continued. The book is 90 percent Talking About Itself, and I'm grateful that people continue to respect my journalistic work, regardless of their opinion of me.
Chega is the result of a wave of populism that has emerged all over the world. Have we learned nothing from history? Not that, and of course at different rates. I dedicate the last chapter to the entry of extremist narratives into schools and colleges, and how the Chega narrative is contaminating, and making complicit, a generation that has a great distance from the 25th of April. Based on testimonies from teachers and other professionals, I report the increasingly frequent presence of students in classrooms giving the Nazi salute, some who quoted parts of ÔÇťMein KampfÔÇŁ, or adopted simplistic narratives, most of them completely false and based on the misinformation that is widespread. These are young people who are bringing things to the classroom that we thought were already resolved. But this also happens because something failed at school, and perhaps Portugal is not unique in this regard. Perhaps, in part, it justifies the rise of these narratives and movements. I also know, from access to Chega's vast internal documentation over the years, that teachers are one of the professional classes with the greatest representation in Chega's activism, from basic education to academia. Here we have factors that are generating a very worrying situation in relation to the entry of extremist ideas into a universe that, in many cases, still does not even vote. One of the ideas that comes out of your book is that we cannot talk about a Chega-type voter. It's not easy for me [to make this definition]. I don't know, at the moment, who the typical Chega voter is because it varies greatly depending on geographic location and political orientations. In other words, Chega attracted everything and a pair of boots, so to speak. Initially, he sought people from the parties that were closest to him ideologically, namely the PSD and the CDS. I went a lot into the field, interviewed hundreds of them, and it is now verifiable with studies carried out on electoral behavior that Chega managed to attract people from various ideological options in the past, from the PCP to the Left Bloc. There were many traditional Socialist Party voters who switched to Chega. The party is disruptive even in relation to the traditional party system itself. In what sense? I believe that a voter or a member of one of the traditional parties may even disagree with the leader at a certain point or disagree with the program at a certain stage, but if he is in that party and votes for that party, it is because he is minimally comfortable, let's say, 70 or 80 percent, with its ideas. Now, Chega doesn't work like that, and has come to break with all of this. We have to imagine Chega as a kind of restaurant where there is chef Ventura who invents about ten dishes, with those ideas that we know by heart and stir-fry, about immigration, the gypsy community, the chemical castration of pedophiles, and then this electoral universe chooses three or four dishes that are most relevant to them, depending on their personal and professional journey, or their frustrations, or their community, even. For example, it's easy to find people from Chega who don't even agree with Andr├ę Ventura's speech about the gypsy community, but they agree when he justifies the need for there to be no sexual education in schools, because that would only serve to teach children how to masturbate. Then they already believe and think it's a fight worth fighting. Each case is different and I have never encountered a party so atomized and individualized at that level. A few years ago, for Vis├úo magazine, he carried out an investigation into Macanese Vit├│rio Cardoso, linked to the PSD, but seen at Chega demonstrations. Is this an example of someone who approached Chega from a traditional party? Everything I know about him and his public statements, some of which, however, have been deleted, and everything I have read over the years about the public expression of his political thought, makes him obviously a figure closer to Chega than to the PSD, even though this PSD is what it is, without much ideological support. It wouldn't surprise me, therefore, if people like Vit├│rio felt much more comfortable in a party like Chega than in the one where they supposedly join. In the Portuguese diaspora, Chega has also grown. How do you explain this phenomenon, taking into account that we are talking about a party with an anti-immigration discourse? This side is less covered in the book, because at the time there were no conditions to delve deeper into the topic. Chega has, from the outset, seen a great mobilization on the part of some Portuguese businesspeople in the diaspora. In France and Switzerland this is very evident. There are, therefore, big businessmen who put themselves at the service of the party and Andr├ę Ventura, using their resources to get as many people as possible to vote. Chega denied it, saying that it has nothing to do with these organizations, and that they are individual initiatives, but the truth is that even in the legislative elections in Geneva, Chega was the only party that managed to put electoral propaganda in the mailboxes. This reflects another aspect, which is the fact that the PS and PSD have been comfortable, in some countries, with the immigrant vote for too long. In other words, as they usually divided voters among themselves, they did not worry much about the political and media literacy of a vast immigration sector. It is necessary to remember that 90 percent of Portuguese people living in the most important countries for Portuguese immigration did not go to vote, and this is often justified by the fact that they feel that Portugal has forgotten them. And then there is another issue, which is the fact that even though people emigrated in very difficult circumstances to escape poverty, the truth is that with their work they achieved a certain status and today consider that any immigration, even from their Portuguese brothers, is harmful. I noticed this a lot in the Azores, for example, where family and friendship conflicts begin to generate. This, from a psychological point of view, is quite complex to dismantle. Representative Jos├ę Pereira Coutinho, who won the last legislative elections in Macau, compared himself to Andr├ę Ventura. Is the president of Chega seen as a kind of political star who almost serves as a reference for others? Yes. It is already clear that the party would have many difficulties in maintaining the political strength it has today without Andr├ę Ventura, who is someone who has dominated media language for many years, long before Chega. He has an overwhelming command of communication and knows how to simplify, obviously behind many lies and falsehoods. I have been writing this since 2020, Chega is, essentially, a project of personal power. Ventura subjected Chega to the leader's cult, and the party is today, without a doubt, less free than it was at the beginning. With less internal criticism? There were, despite everything, certain currents and people with their own thoughts, who, whether or not they agreed with most of the proposals that the party offered, were capable of taking the stage at a congress and criticizing the leadership. I have serious doubts that this will happen at the next Chega congress. The rulings of the Constitutional Court are overwhelming regarding the lack of internal democracy, and [this happens] out of survival instinct, as Andr├ę Ventura rarely allowed to give [the stage] to other people. There are recent studies that demonstrate that Andr├ę Ventura transformed Chega into a leadership very similar to that of the North Korean regime. You can ask me, if he leaves tomorrow, obviously Chega will remain. It suffers a great shock, but we still have the big question, just as we had in the United States: it was not because Donald Trump was out of the White House for four years that he ceased to exist. Here we have the same problem, it will not be because Ventura leaves that ÔÇťVentura CountryÔÇŁ will cease to exist, it is necessary to respond to the desires and frustrations that generated this ÔÇťVentura CountryÔÇŁ. If this is not done, this ÔÇťVentura CountryÔÇŁ will go looking for another Ventura. Will Ventura be prime minister in Portugal in the next legislative period? With or without an absolute majority? If there is a political crisis again, I am convinced that Chega has a high chance of becoming a Government. But the truth is that there are signs of wear and tear in ChegaÔÇÖs narrative. From the electorate that joined in strongly from the beginning, there are signs that it found other types of motivations. Between the local elections and the first round of the presidential elections, and this was not talked about much, Chega lost around 100 thousand votes. These are very different elections, but 100,000 votes are not lost like that either. And the truth is that Chega had, in relation to the last local councils, very high expectations which, even with three municipal councils won, were not confirmed. The truth is that, if there is no immediate change in the political cycle, Chega will have more difficulty reaching the Government.

item.title: Tracy ChoiÔÇÖs new film ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ premieres today at MGM Macau: ÔÇťItÔÇÖs very difficult to be a director in MacauÔÇŁ
item.description: In ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ, her new film and fourth feature film of her career, Tracy Choi created a kind of alter-ego, through a character who, after studying in Taiwan, lives in Hong Kong while struggling to finance a film. In an interview with HM, the director says she wants to continue making films about women and their experiences. The official premiere of ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ is scheduled for today, at 7:30 pm, at MGM Macau It's been exactly ten years since ÔÇťSisterhoodÔÇŁ premiered, and Tracy Choi probably wouldn't have imagined that, a decade later, the success would be real, with more and more international recognition for her way of making films. After placing a Macau film at the Busan International Film Festival for the first time, with ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ, it is the local directorÔÇÖs turn to bring her new film, the fourth feature film of her career, to your home. The premiere takes place today at the ÔÇťTreasure BoxÔÇŁ room at MGM Macau, starting at 7:30 pm. We spoke to the director before the premiere of the new work, who told us how she started thinking about the film's script during the Covid-19 period. "The story is about a girl who grew up in Macau, who studied in Taiwan and went to work in Hong Kong. I thought about it [the film] during the pandemic, because we couldn't go anywhere and we had nothing to do. I decided to write a script about myself: a girl from Macau growing up, seeing how she grows up and how she finds herself, something like that." The cast is made up of actresses that Tracy Choi has worked with, namely Fish Liew, Jennifer Yu, Elizabeth Tang, Ning Han, Natalie Hsu and Eliz Lao. "One of them has already received several nominations and even awards in Hong Kong, including best actress [Jennifer Yu]. This time, after almost ten years, we worked together again. I think we grew a lot and it was like working among friends. It was a great happiness to be able to work with someone I know well. The chemistry between them is also very good, because they are friends in real life." In the case of Natalie Hsu, she was nominated for the ÔÇťBest ActressÔÇŁ award at the 43rd edition of the Hong Kong Film Awards, while Eliz Lao is a local actress, who even had a film cycle under her own name at Cinemateca Paix├úo. Eliz Lao made films in Hong Kong and China, having experience in television and theater, without forgetting her training in ballet. Regarding today's premiere, Tracy Choi highlights that ÔÇťfilms with LGBT themes, or stories about female growth, are not very commercialÔÇŁ. "Whenever we tried to enter festivals, we were told that the film would not have great sales. Still, even if the film does not have great commercial success, we hope to reach more audiences, and make more people aware of the film", he highlighted. ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ was shot and produced with public funds, and filmed in 2024. Tracy Choi and her team also sought investment in Taiwan and Hong Kong. After launching in Macau, the team is preparing for new debuts in the neighboring region and in Taiwan. Regarding the recent experience in Busan, South Korea, with ÔÇťGirlfriendsÔÇŁ as part of the ÔÇťVision SectionÔÇŁ competition section, Tracy Choi brought back the best memories. "It was a great experience to premiere the film there. It was the first Macau film to be shown at the festival, we had a great night. The public also seemed to like it and we ended up being invited to go to other festivals, and that was important for the whole team."
